Title: Enable a Hotspot Without Touching Your iPhone Using Yosemite Introduction: This tutorial will guide you through enabling a hotspot on your iPhone without physically touching it, using Apple's Yosemite operating system on your Mac computer. This can be particularly useful when your iPhone is out of reach or if you prefer managing certain settings from your Mac. By following these steps, you'll create a personal Wi-Fi network that shares your iPhone's cellular data connection with other devices via your Mac. **Note:** Make sure both your iPhone and Mac are connected to the same iCloud account and Bluetooth is enabled on both devices. Also ensure your Mac has the necessary hardware (Airport card) to support creating a wireless network. I. Check Compatibility A. Confirm that your iPhone runs iOS 8.1 or later by going to **Settings > General > Software Update**. If an update is available, install it before proceeding. B. Verify that your Mac runs OS X Yosemite (10.10) or later by clicking on the **Apple menu > About this Mac**. If needed, upgrade your Mac to the required version before continuing. II. Turn On Personal Hotspot on iPhone A. Unlock your iPhone and go to **Settings > Cellular > Personal Hotspot**. B. Toggle ON 'Allow Others to Join'. A password will automatically generate; keep note of this password as you'll need it later. C. Leave your iPhone untouched now. We'll handle further configurations on your Mac. III. Create a Wi-Fi Network on Mac A. Click on the **Apple Menu > System Preferences > Sharing**. B. Select 'Internet Sharing' from the list on the left sidebar. It should appear grayed out initially. C. From the 'Share your connection from:' dropdown menu, select 'iPhone USB'. D. Check the box next to 'Wi-Fi', then click on the 'Wi-Fi Options...' button below it. E. Enter a descriptive name for your new network under 'Network Name'. F. Choose a suitable security method such as WPA2 Personal and enter a strong passphrase under 'Channel': and 'Security': respectively. Remember this information since others trying to connect will require it. G. Press 'OK' after filling in all details correctly. H. Back at the main Internet Sharing window, check the box beside 'Internet Sharing' on the list on the left sidebar. You'll receive a popup asking if you're sure you want to turn on internet sharing. Confirm by clicking 'Start'. IV. Connect Devices to Newly Created Network Now, any device within range can detect and join the newly created Wi-Fi network using the SSID and password specified earlier. Once connected, they'll have access to the internet via your iPhone's cellular data plan. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Keep your iPhone close enough to your Mac so their Bluetooth signals can maintain communication. - Always remember the generated passwords during setup for future connections. - Disable Internet Sharing when not in use to secure your network and conserve battery life on your iPhone. - Be aware of potential data overage charges depending on your carrier's policies and usage limits. The best places to find your pulse are the wrists, the inside of your elbow, the side of your neck and the top of the foot. To get the most accurate reading, put your finger over your pulse and count the number of beats in 60 seconds. Finally, a third theory was suggested. This comprehensive guide will walk you through the process of creating a professional and visually appealing jewelry portfolio, both in print and digital formats. ### Part 1: Gathering Your Work **Step 1: Select Your Best Pieces** Before you start compiling your portfolio, carefully choose your best pieces that highlight your skills, creativity, and versatility as a designer. Aim for a mix of different styles, materials, and techniques. Remember, quality over quantity - include only your finest work to make a strong impression. *Key Tip:* Keep track of any awards, recognitions, or publications featuring your jewelry; these can be included in your portfolio to add credibility. **Step 2: Document Each Item Thoroughly** Take high-quality photographs of each piece from multiple angles (front, back, side). Use good lighting and consider hiring a professional photographer if necessary. Also, create detailed descriptions including dimensions, materials used, inspirations, and unique selling points. *Guideline*: Make sure all images are at least 300 dpi for printing purposes. ### Part 2: Organizing Your Portfolio This now we'll have a racing XXX this car. [Disney Phone says "oh hi this is Mickey Mouse"] Title: How to Draw a Line Graph Line graphs are useful tools for visualizing trends and patterns over time. They can help you present data in a clear and concise manner, making it easier to understand relationships between different sets of information. This tutorial will guide you through the process of creating a line graph by hand, providing detailed explanations for each step along with key tips and guidelines. ### Step 1: Gather Your Materials To draw a line graph, you'll need: - A ruler or straightedge - Grid paper (optional but recommended) - Pencil and eraser - Calculator (for more complex calculations) Having these materials ready will ensure that you can accurately plot your data points and connect them with smooth lines. ### Step 2: Determine the Variables Decide what two variables you want to compare in your line graph. One variable will be represented on the x-axis (horizontal axis), while the other will be plotted on the y-axis (vertical axis). For example, if you're comparing temperature changes over several months, temperature would be your y-variable and month would be your x-variable. Key tip: Choose variables that have a logical connection, such as time vs. quantity or frequency. ### Step 3: Label the Axes Clearly label both axes with their corresponding variables and units of measurement. Make sure to use consistent spacing and legible font size so that your audience can easily read the labels. Guideline: Use a font size no smaller than 8pt to maintain readability. ### Step 4: Set Scales for Both Axes Determine appropriate scales for both axes based on the range of your data. The scale should start at zero and increase uniformly along the entire length of each axis. Be careful not to exaggerate small differences by choosing too large a scale; this could misrepresent the data. Key tip: If possible, choose scales where the tick marks align with whole numbers or round intervals (e.g., every 5 units). ### Step 5: Plot Data Points Polytheism is well documented in historical religions of classical antiquity, especially those of ancient Greeks and Romans. How to Notate Special Events in Chess Algebraic chess notation is universally understood in the chess world because it doesn't rely on a particular language. With algebraic notation, each square is indicated by a file letter (a-h, indicating which column it's in) and a rank (1-8, indicating the row). Each piece except the pawn is represented with a capital letter. With this information, you can indicate the movement of any piece on the chess board with just a few characters. Some chess moves, however, involve more than just moving a piece. These moves are notated in special ways. Indicating captures in chess To indicate a capture with chess notation, you use an x along with the name of the square where the capture is made. Imagine a game that begins with white moving a pawn to e4, followed by a black pawn move to e5. White then moves another pawn to d4. The notation looks like this: d4White and black face off, and a white pawn steps out to d4. Black can capture the white pawn on d4 with its pawn. This capture is written as follows, and the following figure illustrates it. d4 exd (or 2. ...exd4, which is more precise) Either notation is correct, because the black pawn can only capture on that one square on the d-file. You may also have noticed that only the file (e), not the file-rank (e5), of the attacking pawn is given. You can leave off the rank because black has just one pawn on this e-file, so the rank is understood. Noting a castle in chess Castling is the only time in chess when the king can move more than one square. You may castle on the kingside or on the queenside, but the rule is the same: The king moves two unoccupied squares to the right or left, and the rook slides around the king to occupy the adjacent square on the king's opposite side. The notation for castling on the kingside is 0-0; the notation for castling on the queenside is 0-0-0. When white castles on the kingside, then, it is written like this: 0-0White castles on the kingside. Recording a pawn promotion You may play lots of games without ever promoting a pawn, but if you do end up moving a pawn all the way across the board, you'll need to be able to write the move down correctly. You notate the promotion of a pawn by adding the piece designation to the move. For example, if on your 40th move you play your pawn to the eighth rank on the b-file and promote it to a queen, you write 40. b8Q. If you promote to a bishop, you write 40. b8B. Accounting for ambiguities in chess notation Sometimes, two pieces can capture on, or move to, the same square. (This situation doesn't happen very often, but when it does, the pesky knights are usually the culprits.) In the following figure, two knights can move to the same square, d2. Chess players solve that potential dilemma by adding the file to the piece designation, as in N/bd2. This notation means that the knight standing on the b-file is the one that moves to d2. So what do you write if two knights on the b-file can move to d2, as in the next figure? To indicate which knight moves in that case, write the entire designation of the originating square: N/b3d2. Or for clarity's sake, you can add a hyphen: N/b3-d2. This notation means that the knight on b3 moves to d2. [holds up a plate from the container] On that going on that choo, choo train they got. [then both Ross and Dad understood him and Ross went off to get him blocks.] What the sharp or flat signs mean is that in that particular key, the notes that will be written in that line or space will be played as sharps or flats. For example, there are no sharps or flats in C major, therefore no designations at the beginning of the score. However, let's move up one fifth, to G Major. In G Major, there is one sharped note, F#, so at the beginning of a piece written in G Major, there will be a sharp sign on the F line of the staff. Moving up one more fifth, to the key of D Major, there are two sharps, F# and C#. So, a score in D Major will have sharp signs on the F line and in the C space at the beginning. This is done so the composer does not have to write in the sharp or flat sign every time that note is played in the piece. It is up to the musician to remember that in the key they are in, all those notes will be played sharp or flat UNLESS otherwise designated as a natural. Out of the loop? Weekly Trip to the Library There is no better time to learn about apple trees then the start of autumn, which is apple harvest season. The Seasons of Arnold's Apple Tree by Gail Gibbons is about a boy named Arnold who has his own apple tree. The tree is his friend. He watches the tree go through each season, and he explains what is happening to the tree during each season. We will use this book to help teach young readers about the four seasons. This is a great book to read before taking children to a local apple orchard to pick their own apples. Before reading the book ask the child to look at the cover of The Seasons of Arnold's Apple Tree. What do they see on the cover? Ask them what they think the book will be about. Tell them the title of the book and then say with humor, "So this book will be about silly dinosaurs?" Then ask him what they think the book will be about. Ask, "What are the four seasons?" Tell the child to pay close attention to the descriptions and the pictures of the apple tree in each season because they will be doing a project related to the book. Begin reading the book. After reading the story, ask the child questions such as: What did the apple tree look like in the spring, winter, summer, and fall? What was Arnold's favorite season? What was your favorite season? What other fruits grow on trees? Explain to the child she is going to show the different ways an apple tree looks in different seasons. For young children click here to print a worksheet and have the child color it in. For children who can draw and read make your own worksheet divided into four boxes. Write the name of a season at the top of each box. Ask the child to put their fingers on the word spring, then on the word summer, fall, and winter. Click here for more apple printables including an apple dot to dot and word search which correspond nicely with the book. Serve apples to eat the day you read the book. At breakfast you can serve apple pancakes or apple muffins. Serve apple sauce with lunch or pack it in their lunchbox. For snacks simply slice up an apple and let kids dip it in peanut butter. Kids love making baked apples. You can even make an apple crisp or an apple pie for dessert after dinner. Click here for our recipe for candy apples. Title: How to Create a Convincing Fake Vial of Poison Creating a convincing fake vial of poison can be an exciting project for various purposes such as costume design, props for theater or film production, or themed parties. This tutorial will guide you through creating a realistic-looking poison vial using simple materials that are easy to find. Remember, this is purely for entertainment or educational purposes only; never attempt to create harmful substances or deceive others into believing they're real! Materials Required: 1. A small glass bottle or vial (preferably dark colored) 2. Hot glue gun and sticks 3. Black acrylic paint 4. White or silver permanent marker 5. Transparent adhesive vinyl sheet (optional) 6. Ruler, scissors, and tweezers 7. Protective eyewear and gloves Step 1: Gather Your Materials Before starting, ensure all safety precautions are taken. Put on protective eyewear and gloves to avoid any accidents while handling tools and materials. Lay out your gathered items so everything is within reach. Step 2: Clean the Bottle Thoroughly clean the inside and outside of your chosen bottle with soap and water. Dry it completely before moving onto the next step. It's crucial that no residue remains because it might interfere with the painting process later on. Step 3: Apply Hot Glue Design Decide on a pattern for your 'poison.' Common designs include skulls, crossbones, biohazard symbols, etc., but feel free to get creative! Use the hot glue gun to create these shapes on the surface of the bottle. Be careful not to burn yourself. Allow the glue to cool down entirely before proceeding. Key Tip: Make sure your design isn't too thick as it may obscure the view of the liquid inside once filled. Also, keep the bottom part clear for labeling. Step 4: Paint the Bottle Now, take your black acrylic paint and cover the entire bottle, including the base, lid, and even the dried hot glue design. Two coats should provide good coverage. Let each coat dry according to the manufacturer's instructions. Step 5: Add Details with Marker Once the paint has fully dried, use your white or silver permanent marker to add finer details to your design. Things like outlines around the skull's eyes, teeth, or adding numbers or letters can enhance the overall look. Step 6: Creating Labels (Optional) If you want a more professional finish, measure the circumference of your bottle where you plan to place the label. Then cut a piece of transparent adhesive vinyl accordingly. Using a computer program or handwrite, create a suitable label text like "Deadly Nightshade" or whatever name suits your theme. Stick this label carefully onto the bottle. Step 7: Fill With Safe Liquid Finally, fill your now-convincing fake vial of poison with a safe liquid. Colored water, vegetable oil, or corn syrup could work well depending on what kind of effect you're going for. Just remember never to replace dangerous chemicals with anything potentially hazardous! Remember, misrepresenting something as genuine when it's not can lead to serious consequences, both legally and ethically. Always make it clear that your creation is merely a prop. Enjoy crafting your realistic-looking poison vial responsibly! And he spilled milk all over the table in the kitchen. No more no, no no, no no. 'Kay would you loan me some money please? Through the open doorway of a small inner room on the right hand, of a character between pantry and cellar, was Dick Dewy's father Reuben, by vocation a "tranter," or irregular carrier. He was a stout florid man about forty years of age, who surveyed people up and down when first making their acquaintance, and generally smiled at the horizon or other distant object during conversations with friends, walking about with a steady sway, and turning out his toes very considerably. Being now occupied in bending over a hogshead, that stood in the pantry ready horsed for the process of broaching, he did not take the trouble to turn or raise his eyes at the entry of his visitors, well knowing by their footsteps that they were the expected old comrades. The main room, on the left, was decked with bunches of holly and other evergreens, and from the middle of the beam bisecting the ceiling hung the mistletoe, of a size out of all proportion to the room, and extending so low that it became necessary for a full-grown person to walk round it in passing, or run the risk of entangling his hair. This apartment contained Mrs. Dewy the tranter's wife, and the four remaining children, Susan, Jim, Bessy, and Charley, graduating uniformly though at wide stages from the age of sixteen to that of four years-the eldest of the series being separated from Dick the firstborn by a nearly equal interval. A Compact Disc, also called a CD are small plastic discs which store and retrieve computer data or music using light. Compact Discs replaced floppy disks because they were faster and could hold more information. The CDs made floppy disks become obsolete. CDs were invented by both Philips and Sony at the same time, but not together. Sony and Philips did work together to create a standard format and the technology to read CDs in 1982. CDs can hold up to 700 MB worth of data, which is about 80 minutes of music. Mini CDs were also made for special small programs like drivers. CDs that have computer information on them are called CD-ROMs, or Compact Disc - Read Only Memory. The diameter of a normal CD is 120 mm. The middle hole in a CD is about 1.5 cm). In 2003 and 2005, two Macworld editors expressed criticism of the permission scheme; Ted Landau called misconfigured permissions "the most common frustration" in macOS, while Rob Griffiths suggested that some users may even have to reset permissions every day, a process which can take up to 15 minutes. More recently, another Macworld editor, Dan Frakes, called the procedure of repairing permissions vastly overused. He argues that macOS typically handles permissions properly without user interference, and resetting permissions should only be tried when problems emerge. Just your imagination. And some basics: Like paper, and something to write with: Maybe something to draw with, too. Or is she feeling slow? - Is she happy? Or what else might she be feeling? - Why did she get this treat? Not all stories are made-up. Some are true stories. These are called "non-fiction," and they are every bit as interesting as fairy tales and other kinds of stories. Title: How to Get a Verified Account on Twitter Twitter verification is a process that confirms the authenticity of notable accounts. A verified badge (a blue checkmark) next to an account's name indicates that Twitter has confirmed that the account belongs to an entity or individual prominent in their field. This article will guide you through the steps required to obtain this coveted status. ### Part 1: Meeting the Eligibility Requirements Step 1: Be Notable Ensure your account represents a public figure, celebrity, brand, or company. Twitter's verification policy primarily focuses on high-profile individuals and organizations. They look for accounts that represent well-known brands or individuals in music, acting, fashion, government, politics, religion, journalism, media, sports, business, and other key interest areas. Key Tip: Demonstrate prominence by having numerous followers, regular tweets, press mentions, and external links pointing towards your profile from reputable sources. Step 2: Adhere to Twitter Rules Before applying for verification, make sure your account follows Twitter's rules and policies. Any violation can lead to suspension or disqualification from getting verified. Key Guideline: Regularly review your content against Twitter's abusive behavior policy, copyright and trademark policy, and impersonation policy. ### Part 2: Setting Up Your Profile Step 3: Complete Your Bio Write a comprehensive bio detailing who you are or what your organization does. Including relevant keywords related to your profession or industry might help increase visibility. Key Tip: Add location information if applicable as it adds credibility to your profile. Step 4: Use a Real Name Use the real name associated with your identity or brand. For companies, use the official name. Step 5: Provide a Website URL Link back to an official website that clearly identifies you or your organization. It strengthens your claim of being a legitimate entity worthy of verification. Step 6: Upload a Profile Picture A clear headshot for personal profiles or a logo for organizational ones shows professionalism and seriousness about maintaining the account. Step 7: Set Up Header Image Again, ensure it reflects either yourself (for individuals) or your brand (for businesses). Consistency between your header image and profile picture reinforces your identity. ### Part 3: Applying for Verification In the early 20th century, before antibiotics became available, Julius Wagner-Jauregg discovered that patients with syphilis could be treated by intentionally infecting them with malaria. The resulting fever would kill the syphilis spirochaetes, and quinine could be administered to control the malaria. Although some patients died from malaria, this was preferable to the almost-certain death from syphilis. puppy [child vocalizes and mother laughs]. This is a drawing of what the surface of a comet might look like. Click on image for full size The Comet Nucleus A comet nucleus is the very center of the comet. It is solid and is made of a special sort of dust which scientists call "fluffy". They call it fluffy because it is full of holes and could be very light. At this point, scientists do not know whether the nucleus is very hard, like solid ground, or very soft, like a snowball. Scientists are going to use the Rosetta mission to land a probe on the surface of a comet! This and other comet missions will certainly help scientists understand the very center of comets! Title: How to Encourage Your Parents to Buy You a Horse Introduction: Owning a horse can be an incredibly rewarding experience, providing companionship, teaching responsibility, and offering opportunities for exercise and recreation. However, convincing your parents to make such a significant investment may seem like a daunting task. By following this comprehensive guide, you will learn effective strategies to present your case, alleviate concerns, and increase the likelihood of your parents agreeing to buy you a horse. Remember that patience and understanding are crucial throughout this process. Step 1: Research Thoroughly Before approaching your parents, invest time in researching all aspects of owning a horse, including costs, care requirements, potential benefits, and local resources. This knowledge will demonstrate your commitment and help address any initial objections from your parents. * Key Topics to Investigate: + Initial Cost (purchase price, tack, equipment) + Monthly Expenses (feed, grooming supplies, veterinary care) + Long-Term Care Needs (stable or pasture rental, farrier services) + Time Commitment (riding, training, maintenance) + Educational Opportunities (horseback riding lessons, clubs, camps) + Local Stables, Clubs, and Events Step 2: Assess Your Readiness Consider whether you possess the necessary skills, maturity level, and dedication required to care for a horse. Reflect on your previous experiences with horses and identify areas where improvement is needed. Present this self-assessment to your parents as part of your argument for ownership. * Skills to Develop: + Riding Ability + Grooming Techniques + Basic Healthcare Knowledge (recognizing signs of illness, administering medication) + Safety Awareness (proper handling, emergency procedures) Step 3: Create a Financial Plan Develop a realistic budget outlining anticipated expenses associated with purchasing and maintaining a horse. Be prepared to discuss cost-cutting measures, such as sharing resources with other owners or volunteering at stables in exchange for reduced fees. * Budget Categories: + Initial Purchase Price + Tack and Equipment + Boarding Fees or Property Maintenance + Food and Supplies + Veterinary Care and Medications Ben and Mia love to swim in the sea. They have big masks and tubes to see the fish. One day, they see a big boat near the shore. The boat has many boxes on it. "Let's go see the boat," Ben says. "Maybe they have something fun." Discussion Guide - Grades: PreK-K, 1-2, 3-5 About this book Show children the cover. Explain that germs are microscopic, which means that they can't be seen except under a microscope. Point out that for this book the illustrator has made the germs bigger than they really are. Ask children if they think germs have eyes, noses, and mouths? Why do they think the illustrator drew them this way? Tell children to look for the ways people can protect themselves from germs as they read this book. Encourage children to share their responses to this book. (Elicit responses such as, funny, silly, germs do a lot of things, germs cause many illnesses, I can protect myself from germs.) Then have children name some things they learned about germs from the book. " But with all the things that you can do the circus is the place for you. " Do you remember what happened to Bambi? He lived with his parents in a small house. One day, Joe was playing in the garden when he spotted a bee buzzing around a colourful flower. Joe was fascinated but suddenly the bee flew towards him, making him panic! He started to run but tripped over and landed awkwardly in a prickly bush. At this point, Joe's mum spotted him and rushed over to help. She gently brushed away the prickly leaves and took Joe into her loving palm. She gently comforted him and spoke in a kind soothing voice. Mum: "It's ok, Joe. That bee was just a bit startled and trying to get away from you. Don't be scared". Joe: "It was terrible, Mum". Mum: "It's ok. You're safe now". Joe felt much better in his Mum's arms and knew that he'd never forget that terrible moment. Title: How to Get in Front of the Screen on MovieStarPlanet MovieStarPlanet is an online social game where you can create your own movie star and play games, chat with friends, and make movies. One feature of the game allows players to appear on the screen during movies they create. This tutorial will guide you through the process of getting in front of the screen on MovieStarPlanet. **Step 1: Create Your Movie Star** If you haven't already done so, sign up for a free account at <https://www.moviestarplanet.com/> and customize your movie star. Choose a unique look that represents you! **Step 2: Enter the Movie Making Studio** Once you have created your movie star, click on the "Movies" tab at the top of the page, then select "My Movies." Click on one of your existing movies or start a new project by clicking "Create New Movie." **Step 3: Set Up Your Scene** Select the backgrounds, props, and animations you want to include in your scene. These elements are crucial as they set the stage for your character's appearance. Make sure they complement your storyline and enhance the visual appeal. **Step 4: Add Your Character(s)** Click on the "Characters" button on the left side of the screen. Drag and drop your movie star onto the stage from the available characters list. If you need multiple stars, add them accordingly. Position your character(s) appropriately within the frame using the arrow keys. **Key Tips:** - Place your character near the center of the screen. - Maintain appropriate distance between characters. - Adjust camera angles (top, bottom, left, right arrows) for better visibility. **Step 5: Customize Character Appearance** Double-click on your character to open the character editor. Here, you can choose outfits, hairstyles, accessories, facial expressions, poses, and more. Use these features wisely to convey emotions and reactions relevant to the scene. **Key Guidelines:** - Dress your character according to their role. - Select facial expressions corresponding to dialogue lines. - Utilize poses to emphasize actions and interactions. **Step 6: Record Dialogue & Actions** With all the preparations complete, hit the red record button to begin recording dialogues, movements, and other activities. Press the pause button when necessary and resume once ready. Be mindful of timing and ensure seamless transitions between scenes. **Key Tip:** Practice makes perfect - rehearse beforehand to minimize mistakes while recording. **Step 7: Review Your Creation** After completing the recording session, watch the entire movie carefully. Check if any adjustments are required regarding positioning, lighting, audio synchronization, etc. Repeat steps 5 and 6 until satisfied with the final product. **Step 8: Save & Share Your Masterpiece** Save your masterpiece once contented. You may now share it with fellow MovieStarPlanet users or keep it private for personal viewing. Either way, enjoy seeing yourself in action on the big screen! By following this comprehensive tutorial, you should be well-equipped to get in front of the screen on MovieStarPlanet successfully. Remember to experiment with different settings, costumes, and poses to hone your skills further and unleash your creativity. Happy moviemaking! He's staring straight at Nina, isn't he? The cent or "penny" (not to be confused with the English penny sterling) is the least worth coin used in the U.S.. There are several different coins with different cent values of different materials and sizes. There is the penny (1 or $0.01), nickel (5 or $0.05), dime (10 or $0.10), quarter (25 or $0.25), and the much rarer half-dollar (50 or $0.50). No okay you don't like that any more, huh? To see what the graph of y = |x| looks like, let's create a table these values, simply plot the points and see what happens. Whenever you have an absolute value graph, the general shape will look like a "v" (or in some cases, an upside down "v" as we will see later). Let's Practice: - Graph y = |x+2| We know what the general shape should look like, but let's create a table of values to see exactly how this graph will look. |-3|||-3 + 2| = |-1| = 1| |-2|||-2 + 2| = |0| = 0| |-1|||-1 + 2| = |1| = 1| |0|||0 + 2| = |2| = 2| |1|||1 + 2| = |3| = 3| |2|||2 + 2| = |4| = 4| |3|||3 + 2| = |5| = 5| So our graph of y = |x + 2| looks like Notice that the graph in this example looks almost identical to the graph of y = |x| except that it was shifted to the left 2 units. This will be important as we try to make generalizations later in the lesson. - Graph y = |x| - 4 The table of values looks like this: |-5||5 - 4 = 1| |-4||4 - 4 = 0| |-3||3 - 4 = -1| |-2||2 - 4 = -2| |-1||1 - 4 = -3| |0||0 - 4 = -4| |1||1 - 4 = -3| Which makes the graph look like this: Notice that the graph in this example is the same shape as except that it has been moved down 4 units. - Graph y = -|x| In creating the table of values, be careful of your order of operations. You should find the absolute value of x first and then change the sign of that answer. So the graph of looks like: In this example, we have the exact same shape as the graph of y = |x| only the "v" shape is upside down now. Based on the examples we've seen so far, there appears to be a pattern when it comes to graphing absolute value - When you have a function in the form y = |x + h| the graph will move h units to the left. When you have a function in the form y = |x - h| the graph will move h units to the right. - When you have a function in the form y = |x| + k the graph will move up k units. When you have a function in the form y = |x| - k the graph will move down k units. - If you have a negative sign in front of the absolute value, the graph will be reflected, or flipped, over the x-axis. Keep in mind that you can also have combinations that change the absolute value graph more than once. Dealing with a panic attack in school can be a challenging experience, but there are several steps you can take to manage your symptoms and feel more comfortable. Here's a detailed guide on how to deal with a panic attack in school: 1. Recognize the signs of a panic attack: A panic attack is a sudden episode of intense fear or discomfort that reaches a peak within minutes. It may include physical symptoms such as a racing heartbeat, sweating, shaking, shortness of breath, chest pain, nausea, dizziness, and tingling or numbness in the hands. If you start experiencing these symptoms at school, it could be a sign that you're having a panic attack. 2. Find a quiet and safe place: Once you recognize the signs of a panic attack, try to find a calm and quiet space where you can sit down and relax. This could be an empty classroom, the library, or even the bathroom. The goal is to remove yourself from any sources of stress or stimulation that might be triggering your panic attack. 3. Practice deep breathing exercises: Deep breathing can help slow down your heart rate and reduce the symptoms of a panic attack. Try inhaling slowly through your nose, counting to four as you do so. Hold your breath for a count of four, then exhale slowly through your mouth, also counting to four. Repeat this process until you start feeling calmer. 4. Use progressive muscle relaxation techniques: Progressive muscle relaxation involves tensing and releasing different muscles in your body to relieve tension and anxiety. Start by clenching your fists tightly for a few seconds, then release them. Move on to other muscle groups, like your biceps, shoulders, neck, and face, and repeat the process. 5. Engage in mindfulness meditation: Mindfulness meditation involves focusing on the present moment without judgment. You can practice mindfulness meditation during a panic attack by paying attention to your senses (what you see, hear, touch, taste, and smell) or by repeating a mantra or positive affirmation in your head. 6. Seek support from a trusted friend or teacher: If possible, reach out to someone you trust at school who can provide emotional support and reassurance during your panic attack. They can help remind you that you're not alone and that your feelings will pass. 7. Consider seeking professional help: If you're experiencing frequent panic attacks or they're interfering with your ability to function at school, consider talking to a mental health professional. They can help you develop coping strategies and potentially diagnose any underlying conditions that may be contributing to your panic attacks. 8. Create a safety plan: Work with a therapist or counselor to create a safety plan that includes specific steps to take when you feel a panic attack coming on. This could include using grounding techniques, practicing self-care activities, or reaching out to supportive people in your life. 9. Take care of yourself outside of school: Make sure you're taking care of yourself both physically and emotionally outside of school. Get enough sleep, eat healthy foods, exercise regularly, and engage in activities that bring you joy and fulfillment. Taking care of your overall well-being can help reduce the frequency and severity of panic attacks. 10. Advocate for accommodations if needed: If your panic attacks are affecting your academic performance or attendance at school, talk to your teachers or school administrators about potential accommodations. This could include things like extra time on tests, modified assignments, or excused absences. Remember that it's important to advocate for yourself and prioritize your mental health. The Sun is standing still now. Oh, it's still moving through space, and still rising in the east and setting in the west. But the points along the horizon at which it rises and sets aren't changing. The Sun will rise at the same spot tomorrow as it does today, and the setting points will be the same, too. Thousands of years ago, skywatchers came up with a word to describe this phenomenon: solstice -- a word that means "the Sun stands still." There are two solstices a year, in December and June. We use the solstices to mark the changing of the seasons. In the northern hemisphere, the December solstice marks the beginning of winter. Over the course of a year, the Sun appears to move north and south through our sky, so its rising and setting points move north and south along the horizon. The Sun doesn't actually move, though. Instead, the change occurs because Earth is tilted on its axis. At the December solstice, the north pole tilts away from the Sun, so the Sun appears farthest south for the year. But at the June solstice, the north pole tilts toward the Sun, so the Sun stands highest in the north for the year. The exact time of this year's December solstice is 6:04 a.m. Central Time tomorrow. Earth's back-and-forth nodding has one other important effect. From here in the north, the Sun is in view for less time at the December solstice than at any other time of the year. That makes the solstice the shortest day of the year. Script by Damond Benningfield, Copyright 2008 For more skywatching tips, astronomy news, and much more, read StarDate magazine. The BEST or the Brihanmumbai Electricity Supply and Transport is Mumbai's public transport service and electricity provider. The government owned organisation, which was set up in 1873, operates one of India's largest bus fleets. Originally setup as a tramway company, it branched out into supplying electricity to the city in 1905, and later into operating buses in 1926. The BEST is run by the city's municipality as an autonomous body. [Brian was loading boxes into the back of the Toyota] You need some stuff with a little bit more substance, my friend. Tutorial: How to Dye Armor in Minecraft Pocket Edition Minecraft Pocket Edition (PE) allows players to customize their armor by dyeing it different colors. This can be a fun way to personalize your character and express yourself in the game. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to dye armor in Minecraft PE. Step 1: Gather Materials Before you can dye your armor, you will need to gather a few materials. You will need: * The type of armor you want to dye (leather only) * A cauldron filled with water * The dye of your choice Key Tip: Only leather armor can be dyed in Minecraft PE. Other types of armor, such as iron or diamond, cannot be dyed. Step 2: Place the Cauldron Once you have gathered all of the necessary materials, you will need to place the cauldron in the game world. To do this, open your inventory and select the cauldron. Then, tap on the ground where you want to place the cauldron. Step 3: Fill the Cauldron with Water With the cauldron placed, you now need to fill it with water. To do this, hold a bucket of water in your hand and tap on the cauldron. The water from the bucket will be transferred into the cauldron. Key Guideline: Make sure that the cauldron is full before moving on to the next step. If there isn't enough water in the cauldron, you won't be able to dye your armor. Step 4: Add the Dye Now that the cauldron is filled with water, you can add the dye. Hold the dye in your hand and tap on the cauldron. The water in the cauldron will change color to match the dye. Key Tip: You can mix different dyes together to create new colors. For example, if you combine red and yellow dye, you will get orange dye. [gives book to Maisie. Let me try my best to create an educational piece based on the number puzzle mentioned in the webpage extract. This will be suitable for grade-school students who have learned about addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division. --- **The Number Puzzle Challenge:** Here's a fun number puzzle for you to try! See if you can figure it out using clues given below. Once you think you know the answer, ask an adult or older sibling to confirm your findings. Good luck! * There are two mystery numbers ($a$ and $b$) with a difference of 3 between them. That means one number is three more than the other number. Symbolically we write this relationship as follows: $$ b - a = 3$$ * The square of the bigger number minus the square of the smaller number equals 51. Mathematically speaking, this translates to: $$ b^2 - a^2 = 51$$ Now see if you can discover the values of these two unknown numbers while keeping both equations in mind! You may want to jot down some ideas and calculations to keep track of progress. --- **Solving the Mystery Numbers:** If you need guidance or would like to compare answers, read further. But remember, solving problems yourself leads to better understanding! Let us first rewrite equation (1) as follows: $$\begin{align} b &= a + 3 \end{align}$$ Next, substitute the expression for $b$ into the second equation: $$\begin{align*} (a + 3)^2 - a^2 &= 51 \\ \text{(Expanding)} \qquad a^2 + 6a + 9 - a^2 &= 51 \\ \text{(Combining like terms)}\quad 6a + 9 &= 51 \\ \text{(Subtracting 9 from both sides)} \quad 6a &= 42 \\ \text{(Dividing both sides by 6)} \quad a &= 7 \end{align*}$$ Great job! Now, recall our initial equation relating $a$ and $b$: $$\begin{align} b &= a + 3 \\ &= 7 + 3\\ &= 10 \end{align}$$ So the two mystery numbers are 7 and 10! Indeed, when checking our results against the original conditions, everything checks out: $$\begin{align} b - a &= 10 - 7 \\ &= 3 \\ b^2 - a^2 &= 10^2 - 7^2 \\ &= 100 - 49 \\ &= 51 \end{align}$$ [has walked over to the trash barrel and attempted to throw the Kleenex away] In a frenzy she rushed on, tearing her dress, her hands, her hair. Violence of some kind was imperative. All at once a pale gleaming open space, shimmering under the stars, lay before her. It was water. Deep Lake! Graphing Logarithmic Functions Graphing Logarithmic Functions Graphing Logarithmic Functions Use this menu to view and help create subtitles for this video in many different languages. You'll probably want to hide YouTube's captions if using these subtitles. - We are asked to graph y is equal to log base 5 of x - and just to remind us what this is saying, - this is saying that y is equal to the power that I have to raise 5 to, to get to x. - Or if i would write this logarithmic equation as an exponential equation - 5 is my base - y is the exponent that i have to raise my base to, - and then x is what i get when I raise 5 to the yth power. - So anther way of writing this equation would be - 5 to the yth power is going to be equal to x. - These are the same thing. - Here we have y as a function of x, here we have x as a function of y. - But they're really saying the exact same thing: - "Raise 5 to the yth power to get x". - But when you put this as a logarithm, you are saying: - "To what power do I have to raise 5 to, to get x? - Well, I have to raise it to y." - Here, what do I get when I raise 5 to the yth power? I get x. - Now that that is out of the way, - let's make ourselves a little table - that we can use to plot some points, - and we can connect the dots to see what this curve looks like. - So let me pick some Xs and some Ys. - And in general we want to pick some numbers - that give us some nice, round answers. - Some nice, fairly simple numbers for us to deal with - so we don't have to get a calculator. - And so in general, you want to pick x values, - you want to pick x values where the power that you have to raise 5 to to get that x value - is a pretty straightforward power. - Or another way to think about it is, you could just think about the different y values - that you want to raise 5 to the power of - and then you can get you x values. - So we could actually think about this one to come up with our actual x values. - But we want to be clear that when we express it like this, - the independent variable is x and the dependent variable is y. - We might just look at this one to pick some nice x's that give us nice, clean answers for y. - So what happens, here I'm actually going to fill out the y first - Just so that we get nice, clean x's. - So let's say that we are going to raise 5 to the --I'm going to pick some new colours-- - to the negative 2 power, -- and let me do some other colours -- - negative 1, zero, 1, and I'll do one more, and then 2. - So once again, this is a little nontraditional - where I'm filling in the dependent variable first, - but the way that we have written it over here, ...... - it's easy to to find out what the independent variable must be for this logarithmic function. - So, what x gives me the y of negative 2? - What does x have to be for y to be equal to -2? - Well, 5 to the negative 2 power is going to be equal to x, - so 5 to the negative 2, is 1 over 25, so we get 1/25. - So another way, if we go back to this earlier one, - if we say log, base 5, of 1/25. - What power do I have to raise 5 to to get 1/25? - Well, I have to raise it to the negative 2 power. - Or you could say 5 to the negative 2 is equal to 1/25. - These are saying the exact same thing. - Now, let's do another one. - What happens when I raise 5 to the negative 1 power? - Well, I get one fifth. For this original one over there, - we are just saying that log base 5 of 1/5, you want to be careful - this is saying: "what power do I have to raise 5 to, in order to get 1/5?" - Well, I have to raise it to the negative 1 power. - Here, what happens when I take 5 tot the 0 power? I get 1. - And so this relationship is saying the same thing as log, base 5, of 1, - what power do I have to raise 5 to to get 1? - Wel, I've just got to raise it to the 0 power. - Let's... what happens when I raise 5 tot the first power? - Well, I get 5. - So if you go over here, that is just saying, what power do I have to raise 5 to to get 5? - Well, I have to just raise it to the 1st power. - And then finally, if I take 5 squared, I get 25. - So if you look at it from the logarithm point of view, you say - what power do I have to raise 5 to to get to 25? - Well, I have to raise it to the second power. - So, I kind of took the inverse of the logarithmic function. I wrote it as an exponential function. - I switched the dependent and independent variables. - So I could pick, or derive, nice clean x's that would give me nice, clean y's. - Now, with that out of the way, but I do want you to remind, - I could have just picked random numbers over here, - but then I probably would have gotten less clean numbers over here, so I would have had to use a calculator. - The only reason why I did it this way, is so I get nice clean results that I can plot by hand - So let's actually graph it. - So the y's go between -2 and 2, - the x's go from 1/25 all the way to 25 - So let's graph it. - So that is my y-axis, and this is my x-axis. - So I'll drw it like that, that is my x-axis and then the y's - start at zero and then you get to positive 1, positive 2, - and then you have -1, -2 and then on the x-axis it is all positive - and I'll let you think about whether the domain here is, well we can think about it, - Is a logarithmic function defined for an x that is not positive? - Is there any power that I could raise 5 to so that I would get 0? - No. You could raise 5 to an infinitely negative power to get a very very small number - that approaches 0, but you can never get- - there is no power that you can raise 5 to to get 0. - So x cannot be 0. There is no power that you can raise 5 to - to get a negative number. So x can also not be a negative number. - So the domain of the function right over here- - and this is relevant becausewe want to think about what we are graphing - - The domain here is x has to be greater than 0. - Let me write that down. - The domain here is that x has to be greater than 0. - So we are only going to be able to graph this function in the positive x-axis. - So with that out of the way, x gets as large as 25, - so let me put those points here, so that's 5, 10, 15, 20 - and 25. - And then let's plot these. - ... and is blue and x is 1.25 and y is -2. - When x is 1/25, is it going to be really close to there, then y is negative 2. - So that's going to be right over there. - Not quite at the y-axis, 1/25 .. of the y-axis, but pretty close. - So that right over there is 1/25 comma -2 right over there. - Then when x is 1/5, which is slihtly further to the right, - 1/5 with y = -2. Every healing of injured teammates and every killing of opponents increases a players score and "Honor". Completing the game's missions affects a player's score in a good way and his "Honor" points as well. The score is saved in the player's account that was being used. Some servers only allow players with certain Honor levels to join. The most common is to not let anyone with less than 10 Honor in because this is the base level that everyone starts with. Some also do not let players with too much Honor in. The developers say that "Honor" is based on the values of the Army: "Loyalty, Duty, Respect, Selfless Service, Honor, Integrity and Personal Courage or Leadership". Its main purpose in the game is stop "team-killing", which is a problem in a lot of online first-person shooters (see team killer). Bro you keep that up you're going to go to the bathroom okay? [mother hands Nina a small pile of Christmas cards] It is a common misconception that George Washington had wooden teeth, as false teeth. He did, however, try many different ways to replace his teeth, including having teeth carved from elk's teeth or ivory. Ivory and bone both have hairline fractures in them, which normally cannot be seen, but started to darken due to Washington's use of wine. The darkened, thin fractures in the bone made the lines look like the grain in a piece of wood. George Washington's teeth started falling out when he was about 22 years old, and he had only one tooth left by the time he became president. It was difficult for him to talk or to eat. At one time, he had false teeth with a special hole so the one tooth he still had could poke through. He tried to keep them smelling clean by soaking them in wine, but instead they became mushy and black. In 1796, a dentist had to pull out George Washington's last tooth, and he kept his tooth in a gold locket attached to his watch chain. When the time came for the president to have his portrait painted, cotton was pushed under his lips to make him look as if he had teeth. The cotton made his mouth puff out, as is seen on the picture on the US $1 bill. DI'n't slide out the back very well though. Who told you it was a stegosaurus? We're going to have a huge pen. Pimple popping irritates skin, can make the bacteria spread, and can cause scars. If you must pop a pimple yourself, it is advised to apply warm water to the area before popping it and to wash your hand thoroughly before beginning so as to avoid spreading bacteria via the hands. "Why do you want to kiss the pine tree?" "Because I like the pine tree. And I like you, Ben," Anna says. She gives the pine tree a kiss. Then she gives Ben a kiss on the cheek. Ben feels his face get hot. He smiles. He likes Anna, too. He gives the pine tree a kiss. Then he gives Anna a kiss on the cheek. They hear a voice. It is Anna's mom. She is holding a basket. She has orange juice and orange cookies for them. "Anna, Ben, come here. It is time for a snack," Anna's mom says. "Okay, mom," Anna says. She lets go of the pine tree and Ben. She runs to her mom. "Okay, mom," Ben says. He lets go of the pine tree and Anna. He runs to her mom. They sit on a blanket and drink orange juice and eat orange cookies. They share with the pine tree. Shark eyes are similar to the eyes of other vertebrates, including similar lenses, corneas and retinas. Their eyesight is well adapted to the marine environment. They can contract and dilate their pupils, like humans, something no teleost fish can do. A tissue behind the retina reflects light back, thereby increasing sight in darker waters. It is being built on Geoje Island, South Korea. It is 488m long and has the internal capacity greater than the total volume of four large aircraft carriers. What it will do is collect natural gas off the coast of Australia, and liquify it. When liquified, the hydrocarbon takes up 600 times less space than its gas. Smaller tankers will take the liquid gas to its buyers. The ship will do liquifying and temporary storage, which is usually done on land. Shell believes this justifies the cost of the ship. The first part is here: Design Time Errors In the last part, you added some code to a button. All the code does is try and count how many times the letter "g" appeared in the word "Debugging". We saw that the programme failed to do this, and came up with the answer zero. To help you find out what went wrong, there is a tool in VB .NET called a Breakpoint. Let's see what this is, and how to use Breakpoints. A breakpoint is like a note to VB.NET to stop your programme at a particular place. You add one to your code by clicking in the margins. A brown circled then appears, indicating where the code will break. The following two images show how to add one: When you click in the margins, to the left of a line of code, a brown circle appears: Notice that the line where you want VB.NET to break is highlighted brown. Run your programme, and click the button. You are immediately returned to the coding window. The place where you put the Breakpoint will now have a yellow arrow on top of the brown circle. The brown highlighted line will now be yellow: The yellow highlight indicates where in your code VB.NET is. To continue checking your code, press F10 on your keyboard (you can also press F11, but this will jump into any Subs or Functions you've set up.) The next line in your code will be highlighted: The yellow arrow, and the yellow highlight, jump down one line. Press the F10 key again. Then hold you mouse on the letter variable. The value this variable currently holds will be displayed: The first time round the loop, the value in letter is "e" (The "c" next to it means that the variable type is Character). If the "e" of "Debugging" is getting checked first, what happened to the "D"? Straight away, this indicates a problem. And the problem is that the Substring method starts counting from zero. So halt your programme by clicking "Debug > Stop Debugging", or click the Stop icon on the toolbar. Change the line in question to this: letter = strText.Substring(0) Run your programme again, and click the button. Or you can use another debugging tool - the Locals window. While your programme is still in Debug mode (the yellow line will still be there, if it is), click Debug > Windows > Locals from the menu bar. You should see the following in the bottom left of your screen: Locals means "Local variables". That is, variables declared in this section of the code. The variables i, letter and LetterCount are showing in the window. The value of these variables is also displayed: 0, Nothing and 0. Press F10 and these values will change. The word "guitar" was adopted into English from Spanish word "guitarra" in the 1600s. In the Middle Ages the word "gitter" or "gittern" was used in England. Both "guitarra" and "gitter" came from the Latin word "cithara". The word "cithara" came from the earlier Greek word "kithara". "Kithara" could have come from the Persian word "seht r". "seh" meaning "three" and "t r" meaning "string". There is also a similar but two-stringed Persian instrument named "dot r". "do" means "two" in Persian. The Indian sitar instrument was named after the Persian "seht r". The sihtar itself is related to the Indian instrument, the sitar. Title: How to Ask Someone Out with a 100% Success Rate Introduction: While there's no guaranteed method to ensure that someone will always say yes when you ask them out, following these steps can significantly increase your chances of success. This guide focuses on building genuine connections, showing respect, being confident, and communicating effectively - all crucial elements in asking someone out successfully. Step 1: Get to Know Them Before asking someone out, invest time in getting to know them better. Engage in conversations about their interests, opinions, and experiences. Showing genuine interest in who they are as individuals demonstrates respect and care, which are essential foundations for any relationship. * Key Tip: Listen actively during conversations. People appreciate when others truly hear what they have to say. It shows consideration and understanding, making them more likely to consider spending time with you outside of your current setting. Step 2: Build a Friendship Friendship often serves as the bedrock of strong relationships. By fostering friendship first, you create trust and mutual appreciation between both parties. When the timing feels right, express your romantic feelings within this context. * Guideline: Be patient. Building friendships takes time; rushing could lead to misunderstandings or discomfort. Remember, authentic connections cannot be rushed. Step 3: Assess Compatibility Consider whether your personalities, values, goals, and lifestyles align. While differences can add richness to a relationship, substantial disparities might cause conflict down the line. If after careful thought you believe compatibility exists, proceed to the next step. Step 4: Find an Appropriate Venue Choose a location where you both feel comfortable and relaxed. Opt for settings free from distractions so you can focus on having meaningful conversation. A quiet caf , park bench, or scenic viewpoint can provide conducive environments. * Key Tip: Avoid loud restaurants or crowded places where deep discussion becomes challenging. Step 5: Prepare Your Approach Plan ahead by reflecting on why you enjoy their company and what makes them special. Consider sharing specific instances where they made you laugh, think deeply, or simply brightened your day. Having clear thoughts beforehand ensures a smoother delivery. Step 6: Communicate Directly but Respectfully Express your intentions honestly yet tactfully. Use phrases such as, "I really value our friendship, and I was wondering if you would like to go out on a date?" Rather than saying, "We should hangout sometime," specify that it is indeed a date. * Guideline: Maintain eye contact throughout the conversation. This conveys sincerity and confidence. However, avoid staring intensely, which may come across as aggressive or uncomfortable. Step 7: Handle Rejection Gracefully Understand that rejection happens and does not necessarily reflect negatively on you. Perhaps the timing isn't right, or they don't share similar feelings. Regardless, respond graciously by thanking them for considering your request and affirming your desire to maintain the existing bond. Conclusion: Remember, every person and situation differs. Adapt these steps accordingly based on individual circumstances. Authenticity, patience, and kindness remain central themes throughout this process. With persistence and adherence to these principles, one can maximize their potential for successful dating experiences. Mommy do you mind if I go watch the news? Creating your signature look on Club Penguin can be a fun and creative way to express yourself within the game. This guide will walk you through the process of creating a unique and stylish penguin avatar that represents you. ### Step 1: Choose a Color Scheme The first step in creating your signature look is choosing a color scheme. This will help tie together all the different elements of your outfit and make sure they complement each other. Consider using colors that reflect your personality or favorite things. For example, if you love nature, you might choose shades of green and brown. Or, if you're into fashion, you could go for bold, bright colors. Key tip: Keep in mind that certain colors may be associated with specific clothing items or accessories in Club Penguin, so try to mix and match carefully to avoid looking like every other penguin on the island. ### Step 2: Select Your Outfit Now it's time to pick out your clothes! Club Penguin has a wide variety of options, including t-shirts, jackets, pants, skirts, and hats. Think about what kind of message you want to send with your appearance - do you want to be cute, edgy, sporty, or something else entirely? Remember to stick to your chosen color scheme as much as possible. Key guideline: Avoid wearing too many patterns at once; instead, opt for solid colors or simple stripes/checkers. Also, consider the proportions of your outfit - balance out larger pieces (like jackets) with smaller ones (like shorts). ### Step 3: Accessorize Wisely Accessories are crucial when it comes to making your penguin stand out. Club Penguin offers plenty of choices, such as sunglasses, scarves, backpacks, and even pets. Use these items to add some personal flair to your look while staying true to your chosen color palette. However, don't overdo it - one or two statement accessories should suffice. Key tip: Some accessories have animations attached to them, which can add an extra layer of fun to your character. Experiment with different options to find the perfect combination. ### Step 4: Customize Your Background Your background is another opportunity to showcase your personality and creativity. You can choose from pre-made backgrounds or create your own by uploading a picture. Make sure the background complements your outfit and doesn't clash with any of its colors. Key guideline: If you decide to upload a custom background, ensure that it meets Club Penguin's community standards and does not contain any inappropriate content. ### Step 5: Perfect Your Poses Poses are a great way to bring your penguin to life and express themselves further. There are various poses available in Club Penguin, ranging from casual stances to action shots. Try experimenting with different combinations until you find the perfect fit for your signature look. Key tip: Consider coordinating your pose with your accessory or background - for instance, if you're holding a guitar, strike a rockstar pose! ### Step 6: Save and Share Your Creation Once you've put together your signature look, make sure to save it so you can easily replicate it whenever you want. Don't forget to share your creation with friends and fellow players - who knows, maybe they'll be inspired by your unique style! Key guideline: Always remember to respect other players' styles and opinions while sharing your own looks. Everyone has their interpretation of "fashion," so keep the conversation lighthearted and positive. When General Franco died and democracy was restored, Madrid became more prosperous. A person who commits murder is called a murderer. The legal definition of "murder" and "manslaughter" may be different in different countries, and is very much argued on: for example, killing in war is not usually called "murder" by those who fight in the war. Killing in self defense (if people being attacked with death seeming imminent kill the attacker, or someone kills an intruder who has burglarized their dwelling) is usually not "murder", but sometimes manslaughter. Many countries do not even have manslaughter as a legal concept, and only have murder; the countries that do not have manslaughter charge with murder instead. Telescopes in space can take better pictures than telescopes on Earth, but for some reason, the pictures were not better than the pictures taken on Earth. They found a problem with the telescope's mirror. The mirror was not curved right. It was only wrong by 2.2 microns (1/50th as thick as a piece of paper). Still, this was enough to cause the images to be blurred. Some said that Hubble was nearsighted. Want to increase your preschooler's learning power? It's as simple as turning on the radio or tapping your toes. When a young child listens to music, plays a musical instrument or even dances, lots of things start happening. The brain starts forming connections that pave the way for learning opportunities such as vocabulary building and math comprehension. Not only that, but music can be fun. So one day when your little one is looking for something to do, try some of these preschool music activities that will get you both moving. 1. Freeze Dance It's as simple as it sounds. Blast some of your preschooler's favorite tunes (or heck, even some of your own) and dance to your heart's content. Then, when she least expects it, yell "freeze!" and stop the music. See what funny position you both wind up in. How long can you hold them? 2. Strike Up the Band Gather up all the music-making instruments you can find. Don't have a piano or a drum at the ready? No worries, homemade is always more fun anyway. Pull out pots, pans, bowls, empty milk cartons -- whatever you have on hand -- and let your little one go to town on them with a wooden or plastic spoon. Fill closed containers like sealable bowls with buttons or pebbles and give them a shake. 3. Draw What You Hear Cue up different types of music on your CD or MP3 player -- pop, kids, classical, country, etc. Give your child some paper and different colored markers or crayons. Start the music and ask him to draw what he is hearing. If he's having trouble, demonstrate. For example, with slower music, you might draw long, loping lines in a dark color. With a faster tune, shorter, sharper angles using a brighter shade. There's no wrong answer here, just what you feel. An alternative would be to play the music outside and hand your little one some chalk to draw on the pavement. 4. Name That Tune Clap or tap out your child's favorite nursery rhyme or song. See if she can figure out what it is. Once she's mastered that game, make it a little more challenging and see if she can do it in less time or less beats. 5. Make-Your-Own Xylophone Fill an assortment of glasses or jugs (or both) with varying levels of water. Line them up in order from least to most full. Give your child a mallet (a wooden spoon works well) and have them experiment with the different sounds. For a pretty variation, add food coloring to the water and make a rainbow. 6. Music Mania! Bring out a variety of songs with a variety of tempos. Ask your preschooler to dance accordingly, encouraging him to speed up if the music is fast and take it easy when the beat slows down. Join in the fun, setting an example on how your child should follow, for instance slowly sliding on your belly during a ballad or doing jumping jacks while a dance song plays. See who can come up with the most interesting move. 7. Singing in the Shower At bath time, encourage your little one to experiment with the acoustics in the bathroom. What happens when you open or close the shower curtain or door? Can she sing louder than the running water? 8. Just Dance (With Props) It doesn't get any easier than this. Clear a large space and crank up the music. Bring out an assortment of fun toys -- hula hoops, bean bags, rubber balls, scarves -- and start dancing! Calculating Porosity ============== Porosity is a crucial concept in various fields such as materials science, civil engineering, and geology. It refers to the ratio of void spaces (pores) in a material to its total volume. This tutorial will guide you through calculating porosity using different methods. Method 1: Volume Method ---------------------- This method requires knowledge of both the sample's bulk volume (Vb) and its pore volume (Vp). Here are the steps: ### Step 1: Determine Bulk Volume (Vb) Determining the bulk volume involves measuring the overall size or dimensions of the sample. For regular shapes like cubes or cylinders, this can be calculated from length, width, and height measurements. Irregularly shaped samples may require water displacement or other volumetric measurement techniques. Key Tip: Accuracy matters here; ensure your tools provide precise readings to get an accurate result. ### Step 2: Determine Pore Volume (Vp) Pore volume is the sum of all empty spaces within the material. One common way to measure pore volume is via liquid intrusion - submerging the sample in a fluid then quantifying the amount absorbed. Another approach uses gas expansion chambers where pressure changes indicate pore volume. Guideline: Be consistent with your chosen method for all samples if comparing multiple specimens. ### Step 3: Apply Formula Once you have both volumes, calculate porosity ( ) using the formula: = Vp / Vb Explanation: The equation divides the pore volume by the bulk volume, giving us a decimal value that represents the fraction of space occupied by pores. Multiplying by 100 converts this into a percentage. From 1945 to 1947, Turing worked on the design of the ACE (Automatic Computing Engine) at the National Physical Laboratory. He presented a paper on 19 February 1946. That paper was "the first detailed design of a stored-program computer". Although it was possible to build ACE, there were delays in starting the project. In late 1947 he returned to Cambridge for a sabbatical year. While he was at Cambridge, the Pilot ACE was built without him. It ran its first program on 10 May 1950. Title: How to Fight Hand-to-Hand Introduction: This guide is intended for self-defense purposes only. It's crucial to remember that violence should always be your last resort. Before engaging in any physical confrontation, exhaust all other options such as de-escalation techniques or seeking help from authorities. Always respect others and their personal space. Never instigate a fight and avoid getting into one if possible. Step 1: Stance and Footwork Maintain a solid stance with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and weight evenly distributed between both legs. This will provide balance and mobility. Keep your hands up to protect your face and maintain constant eye contact with your opponent. Good footwork is essential; slide your feet rather than lifting them off the ground to move quickly and stay agile. Key Tip: Stay relaxed but alert. Tension can slow you down and make you an easier target. Step 2: Basic Punching Techniques There are several types of punches, including jabs, crosses, hooks, and uppercuts. A jab is a quick straight punch thrown with your lead hand (the hand closest to your opponent). A cross is a powerful straight punch using your rear hand. Hooks are swung horizontally towards the side of the head or body while uppercuts aim upward, typically targeted at the chin or solar plexus. Guideline: Practice these punches individually until they become second nature. Speed and accuracy are more important than power in most situations. Step 3: Defense Moves Protect yourself by keeping your hands near your cheeks, elbows tucked close to your torso. Block incoming blows with your arms, deflecting them away from vital areas like your head and stomach. Parrying involves subtly redirecting an attacker's strike so you can counterattack. Slipping involves moving your upper body out of harm's way just enough to dodge a blow without significantly changing your overall position. Key Tip: Don't forget about evasion tactics. Dodging and weaving can save you from taking damage. Step 4: Combination Attacks Combine different punches and defensive moves to keep your opponent guessing. For example, throw a jab followed by a hook or feint (pretend to throw a punch) to gauge their reaction before actually attacking. Remember, unpredictability is key when fighting. Guideline: Regular practice is necessary to ensure smooth transitions between attacks and defense mechanisms. Step 5: Conditioning and Strength Training Regular exercise regimes focusing on core strength, cardiovascular fitness, and explosive power will enhance your combat abilities. Consider activities like running, swimming, push-ups, sit-ups, burpees, shadow boxing, and heavy bag workouts. Key Tip: Consistency trumps intensity. Gradual yet consistent training yields better results than sporadic high-intensity sessions. Step 6: Legal & Ethical Considerations Understand the legal implications of self-defense. Laws vary widely depending on location, so consult local regulations regarding what constitutes reasonable force. Also, consider the moral aspects of defending oneself - causing unnecessary harm or escalating a situation is never justified. Conclusion: While this guide provides basic instruction on hand-to-hand combat, nothing compares to professional training under controlled conditions. If interested in learning martial arts or combat sports, seek qualified instructors who emphasize discipline, respect, and safety. It's a bright color too, isn't it? In about 1503 Leonardo began painting the portrait of a woman known as "Mona Lisa", the most famous portrait that has ever been painted. He continued working on it for many years. It is a small picture, painted in oil on a wooden panel. It shows the face, upper body and hands of a woman. She is very plainly dressed. For a portrait, a woman would usually put on her best clothes and jewellery. Mona Lisa has a dark dress and a fine black veil over her head. Leonardo often left symbols in his paintings that give clues about the person. The unusual thing about this picture is the smile. The smile is the clue to her name: Mona Lisa Giacondo. Giacondo means "the joking one". (Mona is short for Madonna which means "My Lady".) " Potassium ions are very important to organisms. That is why fertilizers have potassium compounds in them. The ions send messages from cells to other cells. It helps biological membranes "depolarize". This means go from a negative to a positive electrical charge. This is needed for muscles to "contract" (get shorter and move things.) It is needed for the heart to "beat" (push blood through blood vessels.) If the potassium level in the blood is too high or too low it can cause death because the heart stops. A few good sources of potassium are bananas, apricots and raisins. These are different things you can take out. Maybe we can do that today and put 'em in our pond. Title: Figure Out What to Write in Your Crush's Yearbook Introduction: Graduation is an exciting time filled with memories and emotions. One memorable aspect of graduation is signing yearbooks. Writing something meaningful and heartfelt for your crush can be nerve-wracking but also provides an opportunity to express your feelings. This wikiHow will guide you through figuring out what to write in your crush's yearbook while ensuring that your message is appropriate, kind, and genuine. Step 1: Reflect on Your Relationship Consider the nature of your relationship with your crush. Are they just an acquaintance or someone you have shared deeper connections? Have you ever had conversations about future plans or personal interests? Understanding the level of closeness between you two will help craft a suitable message. Key Tips: * Avoid assuming too much intimacy if there hasn't been any established; keep it friendly and positive. * If you've discussed specific topics before (like favorite books, movies, etc.), consider mentioning those in your note as a way to show you pay attention. Step 2: Choose the Right Tone Your tone should match the type of relationship you have with your crush. For instance, a casual tone might work best for friends, whereas a more formal one could suit classmates you don't know well. However, avoid being overly familiar unless invited to do so. Guidelines: * Maintain respect and kindness throughout your message. * Stay away from sarcasm or inside jokes that others may not understand. Step 3: Brainstorm Ideas Before putting pen to paper, brainstorm different ideas based on the information gathered in steps 1 and 2. Some potential themes include: * Expressing admiration for their achievements * Sharing fond memories together * Offering words of encouragement for their next chapter Step 4: Draft Your Message Start by writing down your thoughts from step 3. Don't worry about making it perfect yet - this is simply a draft. Here are some pointers: * Begin with a greeting such as "Dear [Name]", "Hi [Name]", or even just "[Name], ". * Make sure to sign off appropriately after your main content (e.g., "Best wishes," "Sincerely," or simply "Yours,"). * Keep your message concise but meaningful. A few sentences usually suffice. Step 5: Revise and Edit Review your draft carefully. Check for grammar mistakes, awkward phrasing, or unintentional ambiguity. Also, ensure that your sentiment comes across clearly without sounding insincere or exaggerated. Key Guidelines: * Be honest and authentic. * Steer clear of cliches or generic phrases. * Double-check spelling, especially when using your crush's name! Step 6: Practice Calligraphy (Optional) If you want your message to look extra special, practice your handwriting or try calligraphy. It shows effort and thoughtfulness beyond mere words. Step 7: Write Final Copy Transfer your revised draft onto your crush's yearbook. Remember to write neatly and legibly. After all, this book serves as a keepsake, so make every word count! Conclusion: Writing in your crush's yearbook doesn't need to be stressful. By following these steps, you can create a sincere, fitting message that reflects your unique connection. Whether your feelings are romantic or platonic, remember to approach the task with positivity, maturity, and consideration. [goes to living room to wrap gifts] You get your hand stuck in there?
