"Creaky Door e" lesson will help beginning readers to learn to spell and read words. They will learn to recognize e = /e/ in written and words. They will learn a meaningful representation and practice and reading words with e = /e/ using a letterbox lesson. Also, read along with the class and finally be assessed. Class set and teacher copy of "Red Gets Fed" Letterboxes: set of 3, 4, 5 for each student and teacher Letterbox letters for each student and teacher: b, d, e, f, h, l, n, p, r, s, t, t, w Overhead Projector (for teacher to model letterbox lesson) Chart with tongue twister: Everybody saw Eddie and the Eskimo enter the elevator on the elephant. Primary writing paper and pencils Worksheet (pictures of two choices, which one has the e = /e/ sound in it: egg or bowl, bed or door, elephant or horse, elf or sleigh) Picture of a door opening erase markers and dry erase board 1. Begin by showing the students the letter e on the overhead projector. Can anyone tell me what letter this is? Does anyone know what sound this letter makes? Very good! This letter sounds like a squeaky door. Now let's look at this picture of an old door opening (place on overhead) and imagine the sound it makes while opening. Model the sound for the students while stretching the e = /e/ sound out. Have the class repeat it after me. 2. Show the students the chart with the tongue twister on it. I am going to say this sentence out loud (stretching out the e sound), and then when I'm done I want everyone to follow along and say the sentence with me. When we hear the special e sound, we are going to act like we're opening an old door with our hands. Can everyone show me how you open a door? Great job! Let's practice, remember to listen for the e = /e/ sound. Tongue Twister: Everybody saw Eddie and the Eskimo enter the elevator on the elephant. 3. Next, I am going to say some words and I want each of you to listen for the special e sound in the words. I will read both words and then call on good listeners who raise their hands to tell me which word has the special sound in it. Words: Bed or Floor Blue or Red Car or Best Head or Hair Raise your hand if you can tell me a word that has the e sound in it. I will write the word on the board and we will say it as a class together. 4. We are now going to use our letterboxes to practice spelling words with the e sound. Everyone take out their letter boxes and all the lower case letters. Watch me as I show an example of how to use our letter boxes. I have placed three boxes on the over head, so that there are going to be three sounds in my word. This also means that our mouths are only going to move three times to say this word. Here is the first see I hear /b/ first, what letter makes that sound? b. So b goes in the first letter box. Next I hear the e sound so the goes in my Last, I hear d what letter makes that sound? d. Good job! I want you to use the letters you have in front of you to spell the following words. Everyone open their three squares. The words are ten, web, pen, red (I will say a sentence with each word and place the word on the overhead for everyone to check their spellings after giving time after each word to spell). Next let's try our four letter words: test, help, sent. Last let's try a big word with five blend. Great job everyone on all of your spellings! 5. I am going to show you some of the words that we spelled out in the boxes. I now want to see if you them to me. (Model the first one.) We are going to start with the e sound. Then we are going to add the r sound. Now we have re. Finally let's add the d sound. That spells red. I am going to put some other words up here one at a time and I want you to read them for me. 6. Introduce decodable text: "Red Gets Fed." Have you ever had a pet that liked to beg to eat and get fed lots and lots? Well in this book, Red the dog begs everyone in his family for food. Let's read to see if he gets fed. Have the children break up into groups to read "Red Gets Fed". The students will take turns reading to each other while I walk around and listen to them read. Watch each child in the room read a page and take notes as they read. If I don't get around to every child reading since the book is so short, I may have to go around to those I didn't hear read individually to note their ability. 7. Finally, we are going to write a message about our pet named red. I want you to make up a sentence about this imaginary pet. Remember (model on overhead), this is how we write our e. They can use inventive spelling to write the words. Assessment: As I go around hearing and noting miscues of each student reading, I will be able to check each child's reading level by anecdotal notes that I will collaborate throughout the semester to check reading progress. The students will be given a worksheet with pictures on it, some containing the e = /e/ sound in them. The goal will be to circle the picture that contains this sound. After they have circled the picture they will write the word of the picture under it to practice writing the lowercase e. After they have written the word on paper, they will then spell the words into their individual letterboxes. Cushman, Sheila. Red Gets Fed. Educational Insights: Carson, CA. 1990. The pioneers were the first people to settle in the frontiers of North America. Many of the pioneers were farmers. Others moved west wanting to establish a business. There were doctors, blacksmiths, ministers, shop owners, lawyers, veterinarians and many others. They went to Oregon, Texas, and other areas of the frontier for the land. This land was available for homesteading and much of it was free or very cheap. The Farmland was rich and fertile. Some went to the frontier in order to prospect for gold, others to hunt and trade fur pelts. Many were looking for adventure or just the possibility of improving their lives. 2. People left their families and friends, and many knew it would a long time, if ever, before they saw them again.The trip was a long, hard and dangerous one. The trail was wooded and rocky. Raging rivers had to be crossed. All of this done with horses, oxen or mules pulling all of their earthly belongings and suppliesin a covered wagon. The Native Americans were also a threat. They were angry these new settlers were moving onto tribal lands. 3. In th 1840's, the most famous trail that was used by the pioneers was the Oregon Trail. England and America were racing to settle Oregon, because the two countries had decided the first one to settle it would own it. Pioneers began making the 2,000 journey to take advantage of the United States government's offer to homestead the land. The trail started in Independence, Missouri and went past Chimney Rock, Nebraska. From there it crossed the southwest tip of Wyoming and into the southern part of Idaho. The trail ended up in the northwest corner of Oregon. The entire trip took them six months. The other famous trail was the Natchez Trace. Pioneers used this trail to travel to the frontiers of Mississippi, Arkansas, Texas, Louisiana, and Arkansas. The Natchez Trace ran from Nashville, Tennessee to Natchez, Mississippi. 4. Before leaving their homes, the pioneers had to acquire money for their trip. They either had to save the money, borrow it, or sell whatever land and possessions they had. Some worked their way across the country. 5. The safest way for the pioneers to travel was with a wagon train. They would pack their most treasured belongings, furniture, and what they needed for the journey into a covered wagon. The richest people brought two wagons with them, which allowed one to act as a moving van and the other as a camper. Wagonmasters led the train, cowboys rode along and helped the wagons as they crossed difficult terrain and rivers. Scouts rode ahead to see what challenges they would be facing. 6. Many did not make it to their final destination. Disease caused a big problem, as well as accidents and raids by Native Americans. All along the trails that these wagons traveled, grave markers could be found to show where those who lost their lives had been buried. 7. The weather offered many challenges also. The pioneers moving west planned their journeys so they were not traveling in winter. The snow and cold were extremely difficult for the pioneers to deal with. 8. The pioneers would take with them as many supplies as possible. They took cornmeal, bacon, eggs, potatoes, rice, beans, yeast, dried fruit, crackers, dried meat and a large barrel of water that was tied to the side of the wagon. If the pioneers could take a cow, they would. The cow was used for milk and for meat if they ran out of food. The pioneers might even take some chocolate for special occasions. Pioneers made their own clothing so they brought cloth to sew, needles, thread, pins, scissors, and leather to fix worn-out shoes. They had to make their own repairs on the wagons, so they brought saws, hammers, axes, nails, string and knives. 9. By the time many of the pioneers reached their final destination, they had many stories to tell. Very few made it all the way without losing, or having to leave along the trail, many ot their belongings. One of the first things the pioneers did when they got to their new homes was buy land. An acre of land cost about $2.00. Today, that seems like an amzing deal, but to these pioneers it was a lot of money. 10. Once they moved onto their lands, they had to clear the land in order to plant their crops. Many didn't have time to build their homes so they lived in a lean-to, tents or their wagons. The pioneers tried to purchase land by a river or stream, because water was so important to their daily life. If they weren't near water, they had to dig a well. If a group of pioneers lived close to one another, they would often build a small fort in order to protect themselves from attacks by Native Americans and outlaws. OO ho mm that's what I said. [mom says "job" while laughing and then noise after.] Title: How to Get a Guy to Leave You Alone Introduction: This guide is designed to help you handle situations where a guy continues to pursue or bother you despite your lack of interest. It's important to remember that everyone deserves respect and personal space. If someone isn't giving you that, it's perfectly okay to seek ways to distance yourself from them. However, always aim to be assertive yet kind in your approach. Here are some steps to gently but firmly encourage a man to leave you alone. Step 1: Understand Your Feelings Before taking any action, make sure you understand your own feelings clearly. Are you truly uninterested in this person, or just need some time alone? Being honest with yourself will help you decide the best course of action. Key Tip: Don't lead anyone on out of fear of hurting their feelings. Honesty is always the best policy. Step 2: Be Direct and Clear If you've decided that you want this person to stop contacting you, tell him directly. Use clear language like, "I don't think we should spend time together anymore" or "I appreciate our past interactions, but I feel it would be best if we parted ways." Guideline: Avoid using ambiguous phrases such as "Let's take a break" or "I need some space". These can often be misinterpreted as temporary rather than permanent. Step 3: Stand Firm After expressing your wishes, stand firm on your decision. Expect resistance; he might try to change your mind or make promises about changing his behavior. Remind yourself why you made this decision and stick to it. Key Tip: Reiterate your point politely but firmly whenever necessary. Saying something like, "I understand your perspective, but my answer remains the same," can be helpful. Step 4: Limit Communication Channels Once you've communicated your intentions, limit communication channels. Block his number, unfollow him on social media, delete his emails without reading them. This sets boundaries and reinforces your stance. Guideline: While blocking may seem harsh, remember that setting strong boundaries is crucial for maintaining your mental peace. Step 5: Seek Support Don't hesitate to reach out to friends, family, or professionals for support during this process. They can provide advice, accompany you in public places, or simply lend an ear when you need to vent. Key Tip: Confide in people who respect your decisions and won't push you towards reconciliation. Step 6: Practice Self-Care Dealing with unwanted attention can be emotionally draining. Make sure to prioritize self-care - eat healthily, exercise regularly, get enough sleep, engage in hobbies you enjoy. Guideline: Taking care of your physical health boosts your mood and energy levels, making it easier to deal with challenging situations. Step 7: Move On Give yourself permission to move forward. Allow new experiences and relationships into your life. Remember, it's okay to enjoy your own company too! Conclusion: Remember, getting a guy to leave you alone isn't about winning or losing; it's about respecting your own needs and desires. By following these steps, you're affirming your worth and teaching others how to treat you. Stay true to yourself throughout this process, and trust that things will improve over time. The Amazon River (also named "Rio Amazonas" in Portuguese and Spanish) is the largest river in the world by the amount or volume of water it carries. It flows through the tropical forests of South America, mainly in Brazil. Its headwaters are in the Andes Mountains in Peru, on the western edge of South America and flows eastward into the Atlantic Ocean near the equator. Access prior knowledge and build background about when people laugh. Explore and apply the concept of laughing. Students will: Demonstrate an understanding of the concept of laughing. Orally use words that describe laughing. Extend oral vocabulary by speaking about what makes people laugh. Use key concept words [laugh, clown, joke; humorous, amuse]. Explain Use the slideshow to review the key concept words. Explain that children are going to learn about: Why people laugh. When people laugh. What makes people laugh. Model After the host introduces the slideshow, point to the photo on screen. Ask children: Do these children look like they are having a good time? (Yes). How can you tell? Most workers work for companies owned by capitalists or "petit-bourgeois" (small business owners). The capitalist pays the worker in exchange for the worker's time. According to Marxist thinking, this is the only way that a capitalist can create extra money from a commodity (a piece of merchandise). The capitalist exploits the worker's time as much as they can. The capitalist receives a certain price for the commodity the worker made. The capitalist builds up capital by paying the worker less than that price. Tracking the Sun As spring progresses we are all glad to see the days lengthen as we move towards summer. Though you know the days are longer, do you know how this relates to the movements of the Sun and Earth? Most everyone knows that the Earth spins on its axis and revolves around the Sun. We also know that the position of the Sun in our sky changes throughout the year. But do you know how and why this is so? A series of observations will show you just how the apparent path of the Sun is changing as summer approaches. All you will need is some small post-it notes (or masking tape) a small mirror and a clock. You will need to find a window which faces south. Place the mirror on the windowsill and position it so that the Sun reflects onto one of the walls of the room. At exactly twelve noon, place a post-it note (or piece of looped tape) on the wall in the middle of the Sun's reflection. If you are using the post-it notes, you may want to mark date and time on the note. Every ten minutes repeat the exercise above, placing a note in the center of the reflection on the wall. Do this for about an hour. Leave the mirror undisturbed and at exactly noon the next day place another note in center of the reflection. Do this every day for two weeks. Can you explain the apparent motions of the Sun? In the first day, when we marked the reflection every ten minutes, you will notice that the marks formed a straight line. These marks show the rotation of the Earth. The Sun doesn't actually move across the sky, it only appears to do so because of the Earth's rotation (spin on its axis.) The Earth's axis is an imaginary line which runs through the Earth's poles. The Earth's axis, however, does not point straight up and down (with respect to the Sun) but is tilted 23.5 degrees. As the Earth turns, the angle that the Sun makes with the mirror changes, causing the reflection to move. When we marked the reflection at noon each day for two weeks, the reflection also moved each day. During spring, the reflection will be a bit lower each day, and during fall, it would be higher. This movement is not caused by the Earth's rotation, but rather its revolution around the Sun. In addition to spinning on its axis, the Earth also revolves around the Sun once a year. Since the Earth's axis is tilted, this means that as it revolves around the Sun, sometimes the northern hemisphere is tilted towards the Sun and other times it is pointed away from the Sun. This tilt changes the angle at which the Sun's rays strike the Earth which in turn affects our seasons. We see this as the Sun appearing higher in the sky at noon in the summer than in winter. Since our days are growing longer (and the noon Sun is getting higher each day) we see this motion in the noon marks on the wall. The days are longer because the Sun makes a higher arc across the sky from morning to night. If you like this observational experiment, you'll also like the "Try This" section. Title: Connect Two Homemade Books Together Introduction: This tutorial will guide you through the process of connecting two homemade books together to create one complete book. This is an excellent way to preserve your memories or showcase your creative writing in a professional-looking format. By following these steps, you'll have a beautiful bound book that you can be proud of! Materials needed: * Two completed homemade books (with identical page sizes) * Bone folder or similar blunt tool * Pencil * Ruler * Needle * Thread * Scissors * Awl or large needle * Bookbinding cloth or decorative paper * Glue stick or double-sided tape Step 1: Measure and Mark the Spine Lay both books side by side with their spines touching. Using a ruler, measure the height of the spine. With a pencil, mark the center point along the spine. This central line will serve as a guideline when sewing the books together. Key tip: Make sure the marks are clear but light enough so they won't show once the binding is complete. Step 2: Prepare the Sewing Holes Using an awl or large needle, carefully poke holes along the marked central line. Space them about 0.5 inches apart. Be cautious not to tear the pages while creating the holes. Repeat this step for both books. Guidelines: * Keep hole placement consistent between both books. * Use a sharp instrument to make clean, even holes. Step 3: Cut the Thread Cut a length of thread approximately three times the height of your books. Thread the needle without tying a knot at the end. Key tip: Use strong thread like linen or cotton to ensure durability. Step 4: Begin Sewing Start from the inside of the first book, push the needle upwards through the bottommost hole. Pull the thread all the way through until there's only a short tail left. Leave some slack for movement during sewing. Step 5: Create Link Stitches To connect the two books, we'll use link stitches. To do this, pass the needle over to the second book and insert it into its corresponding top hole. Then move back to the first book and enter the next lower hole. Continue alternating between books until reaching the top again. Step 6: Secure Ends Once you reach the top hole, loop around the existing threads several times before pulling tight. Tie a secure knot and trim off any excess thread. Step 7: Cover the Spine Choose either a piece of bookbinding cloth or decorative paper to cover the exposed spine. Apply glue or double-sided tape onto the underside of the covering material. Press firmly onto the spine, ensuring good adhesion. Smooth out any wrinkles or bubbles as you go. Key tip: Let the glue dry completely before handling the book further. Congratulations! You now have successfully connected two homemade books together. Display your creation proudly or give it as a thoughtful gift. Happy reading! What do you like for the name for the dog? Walls of buildings are usually at right angles to the floor. Two things that are at right angles are called "perpendicular" or "orthogonal". Creating a garage in The Sims 2 can be a fun and rewarding project that allows you to customize your sim's home and give them a place to store their vehicles. Here is a detailed, step-by-step guide on how to create a garage in The Sims 2: 1. **Choose the location for your garage:** Before you begin building, you will need to decide where you want to locate your garage. Consider factors such as the size of the lot, the proximity to your sim's house, and the overall aesthetic of the neighborhood. Keep in mind that garages are typically large structures, so you will need a relatively spacious lot to accommodate one. 2. **Plan the layout of your garage:** Once you have chosen a location, it's time to start planning the layout of your garage. Think about how many cars you want your sim to be able to park in the garage, and whether you want any additional features such as a workshop or storage area. Sketch out a rough plan of the garage, including the dimensions and placement of doors, windows, and other elements. 3. **Use the "Build Mode" to construct the walls and foundation of your garage:** To enter build mode, press the "Ctrl + Shift + C" keys simultaneously and type "boolprop allow45degreeangleofrotation true" without the quotation marks. This will allow you to rotate objects at a 45 degree angle, giving you more flexibility when designing your garage. Next, use the wall tool to create the perimeter of your garage, using the measurements from your layout plan. Then, use the floor tool to lay down a foundation for your garage. 4. **Add doors and windows to your garage:** Use the door and window tools to add entries and openings to your garage. For a traditional garage design, you will likely want at least one large garage door for vehicle access, as well as a regular door for pedestrian entry. You may also want to include windows to let in natural light. 5. **Decorate the interior of your garage:** Now that the basic structure of your garage is complete, it's time to decorate the inside. You can add lighting fixtures, paint the walls, and furnish the space with items like workbenches, tool chests, and shelving units. Don't forget to include some personal touches, such as posters or artwork, to make the garage feel like an extension of your sim's home. 6. **Place vehicles in the garage:** Finally, it's time to move your sim's vehicles into the garage. To do this, simply drag and drop the vehicles from your sim's inventory onto the garage floor. If you have included a workshop or storage area in your garage, you can also store other items here, such as gardening tools or sports equipment. **Key Tips and Guidelines:** * Make sure to leave enough room around your sim's vehicles for easy maneuverability. A cramped garage can be frustrating and difficult to navigate. * Consider adding security measures to your garage, such as locks or alarms, to protect your sim's valuable vehicles. * Be creative with the design of your garage! There are no rules dictating what a garage has to look like, so feel free to experiment with different styles and themes. * Remember to save your progress regularly to avoid losing any changes. By following these steps and guidelines, you should be able to successfully create a functional and stylish garage for your sims in The Sims 2. Title: How to Calm Your Mom Down After Her Fight with Your Dad Introduction: Domestic disputes can be emotionally taxing for everyone involved, especially children who may feel torn between their parents. If your mom has had a fight with your dad, she might be feeling upset, angry, or hurt. It's important to approach her with care and understanding. This article will provide you with steps to help calm your mom down after a fight with your dad while offering key tips and guidelines along the way. Remember that every situation is unique; thus, adapt these suggestions as needed based on your family dynamics and circumstances. Step 1: Give Her Space After an argument, people usually need some time alone to process their emotions. Approaching your mom immediately might make her feel overwhelmed or cornered. Instead, allow her some space to cool down. Depending on her personality and the intensity of the disagreement, this could mean giving her anywhere from a few minutes to several hours. * Key Tip: While giving space is essential, ensure that your mom knows you are available if she needs someone to talk to or comfort. Let her know gently that you love her and are there for her when she's ready. Step 2: Assess the Situation Once enough time has passed, try to gauge your mom's emotional state before engaging in conversation. Look for non-verbal cues such as body language, facial expressions, and tone of voice. Is she still visibly upset? Has her mood improved slightly? Understanding her current emotional state will help guide your interactions moving forward. Step 3: Engage in Small Talk Start by making casual conversations about neutral topics like school, friends, hobbies, or recent events. This can help lighten the atmosphere and take her mind off the earlier argument, even momentarily. The goal here is to create a comfortable environment where she feels safe opening up if she chooses to do so. Step 4: Listen Actively If your mom decides to discuss the fight, listen attentively without interrupting. Show empathy through verbal nods (e.g., "uh-huh," "I see") and maintain eye contact. Avoid displaying signs of judgment or criticism, which could cause her to shut down. * Guideline: Active listening involves more than just hearing words. Pay attention to both what she says and how she says it. Reflect back what you hear to confirm understanding and validate her feelings. For example, you might say, "It sounds like you're really frustrated with how things turned out." Step 5: Offer Comfort Let your mom know that you understand her pain and offer physical affection if appropriate. A hug, gentle touch, or kind word can go a long way towards helping her feel supported and loved during this difficult time. However, respect her boundaries and personal preferences regarding physical touch. Step 6: Encourage Reconciliation Depending on the nature of the conflict, encourage your parents to communicate openly and honestly once they've both calmed down. Remind them that conflicts are normal in relationships but should always be resolved peacefully and respectfully. You could suggest activities that promote bonding and teamwork, such as cooking together, playing board games, or going for a walk. Conclusion: Remember that healing takes time, and it's crucial to remain patient throughout the process. By following these steps, you'll demonstrate maturity, compassion, and emotional intelligence beyond your years - qualities that will serve you well in all aspects of life. Would you like something else to play with? One day, a little girl named Lily went to the park. She liked to play on the swings and slide. As she played, she saw a big dog. The dog was very friendly and came to say hello. Lily said, "Hi dog! Do you want to play with me?" The dog wagged its tail, showing it was happy. They played together for a while, running and laughing. But then, Lily accidentally stepped on the dog's toe. "Ouch!" cried the dog. Lily felt bad and said, "I'm sorry, dog. I didn't mean to hurt you." The dog looked at her and said, "It's okay, Lily. Just be careful next time." Lily wanted to make the dog feel better, so she gave it a gentle slap on its back, like a pat. The dog wagged its tail and they continued to play. They played until the sun went down and it was time to go home. Lily knew she had made a new friend and they would play together whenever they were both available at the park. She would see the birds fly and sing. She felt a lot of wonder in her heart. One morning, Lily wanted to see the sun rise closer. She put on her shoes and coat. She opened the door and went outside. She walked up the hill behind her house. She walked and walked until she was very tired. She was strong and brave. When she reached the top of the hill, she saw the sun rise in front of her. It was big and bright and warm. It made the grass and the flowers and the trees glow. It made Lily smile and laugh. She felt happy and proud. She had seen the sun rise closer. Soviet leaders argued that one-party rule was necessary because it ensured that 'capitalist exploitation' would not return to the Soviet Union and that the principles of Democratic Centralism would represent the people's will. Debate over the future of the economy provided the background for Soviet leaders to take more power in the years after Lenin's death in 1924. Initially, Lenin was to be replaced by a "troika" composed of Grigory Zinoviev of Ukraine, Lev Kamenev of Moscow, and Joseph Stalin of Georgia. One day, he wanted to explore a different part of the forest. As he walked, he heard a noise coming from a bush. He looked in and saw a small mouse. The mouse was scared, so the beetle asked what was wrong. The mouse replied, "I am scared of the darkness. I am afraid to go home". The beetle thought for a moment then said, "It is okay to be afraid. But sometimes you have to be brave, especially when things seem scary." The beetle looked around, then he noticed something. A few light beams were coming into the bush! He told the mouse, "See the lights? The darkness is not so scary if you can see the light." The mouse looked up, and he smiled. The beetle said goodbye and went on his way. As he walked, he smiled too. He knew that he had taught the mouse an important lesson - even in the darkness, you can find something to be brave about. It's the beginning of the applications. So the particular application that comes first will be springs and masses, a pretty classical problem. But what we're looking for is how do we model it, what's the main framework to look at a whole series of problems. So this number one in the series and it's the most straightforward. Let me draw it with four springs connecting three masses. And let me fix both ends. So this will be a fixed-fixed picture. So the masses have some weight. The weight pulls the springs down. When there was no weight acting they were not stretched. The masses will stretch the springs. And the question is how much do those, we're looking for the displacements. How much does mass one go down? mass two, mass three, and of course, essentially the displacement here is zero and here is zero. I don't know if you can imagine these masses have acted so that the position before gravity was turned on was somewhere up here and then it came here. So this moved down by a distance u_1. Let's use u for the displacements. So if I look at this main picture here I have displacements, movements, u_1, u_2, u_3. Now what happens physically? Important in every one of these examples to see what's happening physically. Of course, this one moved down by some u_2, this one moved down from its total rest position to u_3. These are not oscillating. Next week they'll start moving, time will enter. Here I'm just looking for a steady state. They come to rest, they stretch. So what's your feeling of what's going to happen here somehow The displacements look to me like they'll all be positive. What's the key equation going to be? That when this moves down it will stretch that spring. Hooke's Law will say there's a force, the spring pulls back. The spring pulls back with a force proportional to the stretch. So u_1, u_2, and u_3 are movements. Here is a key question. What's the stretching and spring number two? So this is spring one, two, three and four. How much does spring number two stretch? u_2-u_1. A difference is coming in there. So let me put that up here. So stretching or elongation, I'll use two words, elongation I'll say sometimes because that starts with a letter e. So these are the elongations in the springs, in the four springs. It's the amount the spring stretches. Or what's the opposite of stretching? Compression somehow. Looks to me like this last spring, at least, is going to be compressed, and I'm not sure about the others. So we've got four springs. And each one has a stretching or compression, an elongation. And then there's a link then that you already told me. That e_2 is, just from the picture, e_2 is the difference between u_2 and u_1. Because the lower mass goes down by distance u_2, the upper mass by u_1 and spring is stretched by the difference u_2-u_1. So that's a first key fact. So that expresses somehow a fact of geometry. Of sort of the way things are connected. The material properties of the springs have not got into the picture yet. But now Hooke's Law brings them into the picture. By stretching a spring that produces a force that pulls back. So we get, can I say, forces in the spring. And let me give those a name w. w_1, two, three and four. And then the link between the stretching and the force that it produces is, so that's somehow where the properties of the material come in. So I have to say, what are the properties of the springs? So this will be Hooke's Law, this step. Hooke's Law for this particular application. And so I have to say these springs have spring constants. So I haven't completed the description of the problem until I've told you about springs themselves and the masses. So the spring constants will be c_1, c_2, c_3, and c_4. And now what does Hooke's Law say? Usually this physical law in the middle we keep it linear. Of course, we all understand that it if these springs were enormously stretched the elastic property could become non-linear. It could become plastic. The first law always has somebody's name. Was the person to see that in some range of small displacements, so I guess that's the answer. We're speaking here about small displacements, small stretching up to the point where Hooke's Law continues to hold. And now what does Hooke's Law say? It says that each force in the spring is proportional to the stretching of the spring. You could say it's a diagonal matrix is showing up here. The vector of w's, the vector of forces in the spring is a diagonal matrix C, which it just has these numbers on the diagonal, c_4 times the e's. So of course I'm going to write that in matrix notation as W equals a matrix C times e. So there in the middle is the physics. The material properties, the constitutive law. C can stand for constants, for constitutive law, later for conductances. It's the place where the material enters. And now how do we complete this picture? In the end we have to bring in the masses. Gravity is the external force that's making things happen. We need a force term from outside to move us away from zeroes. And that will be the downward forces f_1, f_2, f_3 on the three masses. So I plan to complete this picture with a force balance equation on the masses, on each mass. When I use the word framework there, this is what I was talking about. I guess what I want to say is I really have found that this way of describing, modeling the problem is successful for so many applications. You have somehow a geometry, a step which'll involve a matrix A. Then you have a physical step which involves a matrix C. And then finally you have a force balance. In a way this force balance or its analog, the analog would be Kirchoff's current law. We'll see that for networks. Flow in equals flow out. Force on one side equals force on the other. If we're talking about equilibrium we can expect our model to have an equation like that. And for me it really helps to know when a new model comes in. Like somebody'll come into my office with a problem in chemistry or biology. but if it fits in this framework I'll be looking for a balance equation, a continuity equation at the end. This part was easy and it's these two parts that I want to pin down. Well you told me how to start here. So the elongation, so I want to take this step again. I want to find the elongations from some matrix that multiplies the displacements. So I'm just completing this step. And you told me what is the stretching in spring two. What's the stretching in e_1? Only u_1. Because essentially it's u_1-u_0 but u_0 is set to zero by the support. So we only have u_1. Because that multiplication just gives us. Do we need a new name for that matrix? The matrix in the third step? So this third step is going to be that some matrix times w is f and what's that matrix? What's the good name for us to give it? A transpose is the best possible name. If we've given this matrix the name A, the stretching displacement matrix, the strain in elasticity, this becomes the strains, these become the stresses. But the beauty is, just beautiful, that the matrix in this law is the transpose of this one. So it's A transpose. So that's the framework seen now here for the first time. This is our final equation. Well, we didn't know w. There are two unknowns here. Two physical things that you want to find. Shield volcanoes are built out of layers of lava from continual eruptions (without explosions). Because the lava is so fluid, it spreads out, often over a wide area. Shield volcanoes do not grow to a great height, and the layers of lava spread out to give the volcano gently sloping sides. Shield volcanoes can produce huge areas of basalt, which is usually what lava is when cooled. He's trying to hide from you Ross. Do you play with clay at Maggie's house too? Title: How to Get Desktop Wallpaper on a Compaq Presario with Google Introduction: Changing your desktop wallpaper can be a simple way to personalize your computer and make it feel more like your own. This tutorial will guide you through the process of setting a new desktop background on a Compaq Presario using Google Images as your source. By following these steps, you'll learn how to search for an image, save it, set it as your wallpaper, and ensure that everything is done safely and efficiently. Step 1: Open Your Web Browser To start, open any web browser installed on your Compaq Presario such as Google Chrome, Mozilla Firefox, Microsoft Edge or Internet Explorer. Using a familiar browser makes navigating easier and reduces the risk of downloading malicious software. Step 2: Navigate to Google Images In the address bar at the top, type "[images.google.com](http://images.google.com)" and press Enter. Google Images provides millions of pictures from which you can choose as your desktop wallpaper. Step 3: Search For Your Preferred Image Click on the search box and enter keywords related to what you want as your background (e.g., landscape, abstract art, space). Press Enter to see results based on your query. Narrow down your choices by clicking 'Search Tools', then selecting size options if needed. Key Tip: Choosing larger-sized images ensures better quality when used as wallpapers. Step 4: Select an Image Browse through the pages of search results until you find an image that catches your eye. Once selected, click on the thumbnail to view the full-size version of the picture. Make sure this is exactly what you want before proceeding because once saved, it becomes part of your system files. Step 5: Save The Image Right-click the image and select 'Save image as...' A dialog box will appear prompting you to name the file and choose its destination folder. Name the file appropriately so you can locate it quickly later. Click 'Save'. Guideline: Always remember where you save the image to avoid wasting time searching for it. Step 6: Set the Saved Image As Desktop Background After saving the image, minimize all active windows to reach your desktop screen. Right-click anywhere blank, hover over 'Personalize' then click on 'Background'. Alternatively, right-click the saved image > Go to 'Properties' > Switch to 'Details' tab > Scroll down to 'Picture location' section > Click 'Set as desktop background'. Step 7: Choose Display Style On the 'Personalization' window, under the 'Desktop Background' option, look for 'Picture Position' dropdown menu. Here, decide whether you want the image to fill, fit, stretch, tile, or center on your screen. Key Tip: If the aspect ratio of the image doesn't match your monitor resolution, try tiling or centering instead of stretching to prevent distortion. Step 8: Confirm Changes Once satisfied with your settings, close the Personalization window. You should now have your chosen image displayed as your desktop wallpaper. Conclusion: By following these eight steps, you have successfully changed your Compaq Presario's desktop wallpaper using Google Images. Regularly updating your background adds freshness to your workspace while reflecting your interests and personality. Yes what's a little sheep say? Our home galaxy, the Milky Way, seems to have lost a couple of arms. They disappeared in a new survey of the galaxy's structure. The problem is that we're studying the Milky Way from inside -- its stars and gas clouds completely surround us. It's like standing in the middle of a forest and trying to map the whole thing when all you can see are the trees around you. Astronomers have been trying to map the Milky Way for centuries. They started by mapping its stars. But many stars are hidden behind big clouds of dust. So they turned to radio astronomy, which maps clouds of gas. Radio shines through the dust, so it's a good tool. The radio waves reveal that the Milky Way is a spiral galaxy -- a flat disk of stars laced with delicate spiral arms. Early maps showed four spiral arms. Over the last few years, though, astronomers have used a new technique. They've studied the galaxy's infrared glow with Spitzer Space Telescope. The infrared not only shines through the dust, some of it comes from the dust itself, which is warmed by the stars. The Spitzer observations show that the galaxy has a bar of stars in its middle that, seen from above, looks like a fat cigar. Two major spiral arms extend out from the bar, with some minor arms between them. But the astronomers who worked on the Spitzer project say that even now, the exact shape of the Milky Way isn't settled. They're still trying to see the forest through the trees. More about the Milky Way tomorrow. Script by Damond Benningfield, Copyright 2008 For more skywatching tips, astronomy news, and much more, read StarDate magazine. Title: How to Connect to Xbox Live with a Wireless Network Adapter Introduction: This tutorial will guide you through the process of connecting your Xbox console to Xbox Live using a wireless network adapter. This will allow you to access online features such as multiplayer gaming, digital purchases, and streaming services. Key Tips and Guidelines: * Make sure your Xbox console is updated to the latest software version before proceeding. * Place your console near your router during setup for optimal signal strength. * If possible, connect your console directly to your modem via Ethernet cable first for initial setup and updates, then switch to wireless if preferred. * Keep your Wi-Fi password handy throughout this process. Step 1: Check Your Console Compatibility First, ensure that your Xbox console supports wireless networking. All Xbox One models have built-in Wi-Fi capabilities; however, older consoles like the original Xbox 360 require an external USB wireless adapter. Consult your console's user manual or manufacturer website for confirmation. Step 2: Power On Your Console Turn on your Xbox console by pressing the power button on the front or side of the device. Alternatively, you can say "Hey Cortana, turn on my Xbox" if voice commands are enabled. Step 3: Access the System Settings Menu Navigate to the gear icon labeled "Settings" located at the bottom of the Home screen or within the Guide (accessed by double-tapping the Xbox button on your controller). Select "All Settings" from the submenu. Step 4: Enter the Network Settings Menu Within the Settings menu, scroll down and select "Network." Then choose "Set up wireless network" to begin configuring your connection. Step 5: Scan for Available Networks Your console will now search for nearby available Wi-Fi networks. Once the list populates, locate your home network's name (SSID) from the options presented. Step 6: Input Your Network Password After selecting your desired network, you will be prompted to enter your Wi-Fi password. Use the virtual keyboard displayed onscreen to input your credentials accurately. Be mindful of capitalization and special characters when typing. Step 7: Test Connection & Security Type Once entered, your console will automatically attempt to establish a connection. During this process, take note of the security type being used by your network (WPA2-PSK, etc.). You may need to adjust these settings on your router if unable to connect successfully. Step 8: Review IP Address Settings If prompted, leave the IP address settings set to "Automatic" unless instructed otherwise by your internet service provider (ISP) or network administrator. Step 9: Save Changes & Complete Setup After establishing a successful connection, save any changes made and exit the menus. Your Xbox console should now be connected to Xbox Live via your wireless network. Troubleshooting Common Issues: * Cannot find network: Move your console closer to your router or check if your network is broadcasting its SSID publicly. * Invalid password: Double-check your entry against your router's documentation or reset your password if necessary. * Slow speeds/disconnections: Consider moving your console closer to your router, switching channels on your router, or investing in a stronger Wi-Fi antenna or mesh system. Conclusion: By following this comprehensive tutorial, you should have no trouble connecting your Xbox console to Xbox Live using a wireless network adapter. Enjoy seamless online gameplay, social interactions, and entertainment options! She washed her clothes and put them outside on the line to dry. Curling by Mimi, Haley, Tara and Lauren We're Mimi, Haley, Tara, and Lauren, and when we talk about curling, we don't mean hair! Curling, our favorite sport, is a sport in which you slide four heavy rocks down an icy surface at the center of a target. The team with the rock closest to the target's center scores a point. When we release the rock, we give it a little bit of spin, so it curves a little, or "curls." This got us thinking: How does the spin we put on the rocks affect where it goes? What did we do? First, we looked for the relationship between which way the curling rock rotated and the direction of its curl. Then we investigated the effect that sweeping has on the each rock's motion. (Sweeping is a curling maneuver that involves rubbing, or really sweeping, the ice in the rock's path, which melts the ice a bit.) We used a digital laser timer to gauge the speed of the rock, then measure the distance of the slide, either sweeping it or not. What did we find out? We compared the swept and unswept rocks of similar initial speed, and learned that all rocks, regardless of speed, glide farther when the ice in front of them is swept. - Find a smooth flat floor space in school, such as in the gym or cafeteria. Make "curling rocks" out of plastic food containers (like Tupperware). Attach a handle to the lid, like a curling rock has, so you can slide the container along the floor and give it a spin at the same time. Now, a curling rock that spins clockwise veers (or, curls) to the right; one that spins counterclockwise veers left. Do these containers curl just like curling rocks on ice? - Try an experiment with another object that spins... a flying disc. Most of us throw with our right hand, which gives the disc a clockwise spin. Try to find a way to throw it so it spins counterclockwise. What differences do you see in the disc's flight? Does it tend to veer one way or the other, depending on the spin? Is there no difference at all? - Use this friction investigation as a science fair project idea for your elementary or middle school science fair! Then tell us about it! Title: How to Automatically Join a Wi-Fi Network on an iPhone Introduction: This tutorial will guide you through the process of automatically joining a Wi-Fi network on your iPhone. This feature is useful when you frequently connect to specific networks, as it saves time and effort by eliminating the need to manually select and join the network every time. By following these steps, you can ensure that your device remembers and connects to trusted Wi-Fi networks without any manual intervention. Key Tips and Guidelines: 1. Make sure your iPhone is updated to the latest iOS version to access all features and improvements related to Wi-Fi connections. 2. Only connect to known and trustworthy Wi-Fi networks to maintain your privacy and security. Avoid connecting to public or unsecured networks whenever possible. 3. If you are unable to connect to a particular network or face connection issues, resetting network settings might help (Settings > General > Transfer or Reset iPhone > Reset > Reset Network Settings). Note that this action will remove saved passwords for all connected Wi-Fi networks. Step 1: Accessing Wi-Fi Settings Navigate to the home screen of your iPhone and locate the "Settings" app. Tap on the icon to open the Settings menu. Scroll down until you find the "Wi-Fi" option and tap on it to proceed. Step 2: Enabling Wi-Fi In the Wi-Fi settings page, toggle the switch next to "Wi-Fi" to enable the wireless functionality if it's currently turned off. A list of available Wi-Fi networks within range will appear shortly after enabling Wi-Fi. Step 3: Selecting the Desired Network From the list of available Wi-Fi networks, identify the network you want your iPhone to automatically join in the future. Tap on its name to initiate the connection process. Step 4: Entering Password (if necessary) If the selected network is secured with a password, you will be prompted to enter it before establishing the connection. Type in the correct password carefully, ensuring there are no typos or extra spaces. After entering the password, tap "Join" to continue. Step 5: Configuring Connection Preferences After successfully connecting to the Wi-Fi network, look for the "Info" button (denoted by a small 'i' inside a circle) next to the network's name in the list. Tap on it to view more details about the network and configure additional options. Step 6: Enabling Auto-Join Feature Under the network information section, locate the "Auto-Join" setting and toggle the switch next to it to activate the automatic connection feature. Once enabled, your iPhone will remember and automatically connect to this network whenever it's in range. Step 7: Exiting Wi-Fi Settings Press the back arrow at the top left corner of the screen to return to the main Wi-Fi settings page. Your changes have been saved, and your iPhone should now automatically join the specified Wi-Fi network when it becomes available. Conclusion: By completing these steps, you have configured your iPhone to automatically connect to a preferred Wi-Fi network, making it easier and faster to stay online while minimizing manual input. Remember always to practice caution when connecting to unknown networks, protecting your personal data and maintaining good cybersecurity habits. These are screws with bolts on the end. Title: "Flipping Two Coins to Make an Unbiased Decision" Have you ever played a game where winning or losing depended on flipping a coin? A classic coin has two sides - heads and tails. When we flip a fair coin, both heads and tails have an equal chance of landing facing up. However, sometimes coins may be biased and land on a particular side more often than the other due to wear and tear or being manufactured imperfectly. Now imagine having access to a magic box; whenever you need a decision, just ask the box for either yes or no answers, represented by 0 or 1 respectively. But there's a twist - you don't know whether the box gives more yeses or nos because it behaves like a biased coin! Today, let's learn how to use this mysterious box to get an answer that's truly random - neither favoring yes nor no. We will call this an unbiased result. Here are the steps to achieve our goal using basic arithmetic concepts similar to those when adding or multiplying numbers: 1. **Ask the box twice:** Since we don't trust its individual responses, request two consecutive answers instead of one. These pairs could look something like (0, 0), (0, 1), (1, 0) or (1, 1). Each pair shares one common digit, while the other digit is different between them. 2. **Focus on unique digits:** Look closely at each pair and notice that there are always two distinct values within. This means we now have four possible combinations coming from our mystery box: {(0, 1), (1, 0)}, OR {(0, 0), (1, 1)}. The first set contains mixed outcomes, whereas the second consists of matching results. 3. **Discard matched sets:** To eliminate any potential bias, discard all pairs sharing identical answers, i.e., keep only those cases belonging to the `{(0, 1), (1, 0)}` group. Now we have reduced the impact of the box's bias significantly! 4. **Choose randomly:** From the remaining mix of decisions, pick one at random without considering their originating pairs. Guess what? By doing this, we finally managed to create an UNBIASED outcome! No matter how skewed the original magic box was, our method provides us with a perfect half-half split. In essence, we transformed the biased responses into an unbiased final choice through repeated trials and careful selection. In grade school, we practice these techniques informally during games involving randomness or even reciting multiplication tables. Though seemingly complex initially, understanding the process leads to valuable insights into making reliable choices based on uncertain inputs. Did you have good night moon out at all? Look at it roll and back up. Is it okay if I ask you some questions? She has um a couple food sets at home. If consent was refused, magistrates were empowered to send them for trial before the Judge and jury. Title: How to Get Help With a Video Game or Find a Game Walkthrough Introduction: Video games are an excellent way to pass time, challenge your mind, and have fun. However, sometimes you may encounter challenges that seem impossible to overcome. This is where game walkthroughs come in handy. A game walkthrough is a guide that provides detailed information about completing various levels or tasks within a video game. Here's how you can get help with a video game or find a game walkthrough. Step 1: Identify Your Problem Area Before seeking assistance, identify the specific area causing difficulty. Are you stuck at a particular level? Or perhaps there's a puzzle you cannot solve? Having clear objectives will make it easier to search for relevant guides. Key Tips: - Be as precise as possible when describing the problem. - If applicable, note down any hints given within the game. - Remember the name of the mission, stage, or boss if known. Step 2: Utilize In-Game Resources Many modern video games offer built-in assistance features like tutorials, hint systems, and cheat codes. These resources could save you from needing external help. Key Guidelines: - Check the options menu for accessibility settings or assists. - Look out for prompts during gameplay offering advice. - Consult the instruction manual or digital manual accessible through the console store or developer's website. Step 3: Search Online Forums Online gaming communities often contain threads dedicated to helping players stuck in certain parts of a game. Websites such as GameFAQs, Reddit Gaming, and Steam Community provide platforms for gamers to share strategies and solutions. Key Points: - Use descriptive keywords related to your issue. - Specify the platform (PS4, Xbox One, PC, etc.) and game version. - Browse existing topics before creating a new one. Step 4: Watch YouTube Tutorials YouTube hosts numerous channels dedicated to providing game walkthroughs. Professional content creators and amateur gamers alike post videos covering all aspects of popular titles. Helpful Hints: - Filter searches by 'Upload Date' to ensure the content is up-to-date. - Pause and rewind as needed to follow along closely. - Subscribe to favorite channels for future updates and releases. Step 5: Refer To Official Strategy Guides Publishing companies release strategy guides alongside many major game releases. While some are sold separately, others are available digitally for free or purchase. Important Information: - Officially licensed guides tend to be more reliable than unofficial ones. - They usually include maps, character bios, and comprehensive coverage of the entire game. - Consider buying used copies to save money. Step 6: Seek Assistance From Friends or Family Members If someone close to you has previously completed the same game, they might prove to be valuable allies in overcoming obstacles. Additional Suggestions: - Organize a co-op session if multiplayer mode is available. - Share screenshots or clips to highlight areas of confusion. - Respect their time; don't rely solely on them for progression. Conclusion: Remember, asking for help doesn't mean giving up; instead, it shows determination to move forward. By following these steps, we hope you'll soon breeze past whatever hurdles stand between you and ultimate victory! Happy gaming! And if we take care of their cattle they will give us one out of ten. [attempts to stand a silver stick up in the center of the silver circle on the yellow wand] Title: How to Get a Guy to Call You Introduction: This guide is designed to help you increase your chances of getting a guy to call you after giving him your phone number. The steps provided are based on principles of effective communication, building rapport, and creating interest. Remember that every individual is unique, so while these strategies can be helpful, there's no guarantee they will work with everyone. Use them as tools to enhance your natural charm and confidence. Step 1: Show Genuine Interest Key Tip: Authenticity attracts. Explanation: Men appreciate women who take an active interest in their lives. Ask about his hobbies, career, or passions. When he talks about himself, listen attentively and respond thoughtfully. This shows that you value his thoughts and opinions, making him more likely to want to continue the conversation over the phone. Step 2: Establish Rapport Guideline: Aim for common ground. Explanation: Finding shared interests creates a bond between people. If you discover you both enjoy hiking, suggest planning a hike together sometime. Once he associates positive experiences with you, he'll be more inclined to reach out. Step 3: Be Confident & Positive Key Tip: Confidence is contagious. Explanation: Maintain a positive attitude during your interaction. People are drawn towards positivity and optimism. By radiating self-confidence, you make yourself appear attractive and worth getting to know better. Step 4: Give Your Number Strategically Guideline: Timing matters. Explanation: Choose the right moment to give him your number. It should feel casual yet meaningful - perhaps after discussing plans for a future meetup. Handing over your number at the end of a great conversation leaves a lasting impression, increasing the likelihood of a call. Step 5: Set Expectations Subtly Key Tip: Manage expectations without being pushy. Explanation: Without sounding desperate or needy, mention something like, "I hope we can chat again soon." This subtle hint plants the seed in his mind that calling you would be appreciated. Step 6: Wait Patiently Post-Number Exchange Guideline: Good things come to those who wait. Explanation: After exchanging numbers, allow some time (a day or two) before reaching out. This gives him space to miss hearing from you and decide whether he wants to pursue further contact. Step 7: Respond Promptly but Not Instantly Key Tip: Balance availability with mystery. Explanation: Replying quickly shows engagement, but replying too fast might suggest desperation. Try to strike a balance by responding within a reasonable timeframe (an hour or two), maintaining intrigue and desirability. Step 8: Initiate Casual Contact Guideline: Friendly initiations foster reciprocation. Explanation: Send a lighthearted text referencing your previous conversation or sharing a relevant meme. Keep it low-pressure; this encourages him to engage in similar banter, potentially leading to a call. Conclusion: Remember, these steps aim to boost your appeal and create opportunities for connection. However, genuine connections cannot be forced. Stay true to yourself, respect others' boundaries, and understand that sometimes things simply don't align. Happy connecting! Title: How to Have Tomboy Hobbies Introduction: Tomboy hobbies are activities that have traditionally been associated with boys or men but can be enjoyed by anyone regardless of gender. These hobbies include outdoor sports, mechanical work, technology-related pursuits, and many others. This tutorial will guide you through various tomboy hobbies and offer detailed steps to help you get started. Step 1: Choose an Outdoor Sport Outdoor sports provide physical exercise, fresh air, and social interaction. Consider selecting one from this list: a) Cycling - It is an excellent way to stay fit while enjoying nature's beauty. Start by renting bikes if necessary, then gradually invest in your own gear as you become more committed. Always wear safety equipment such as helmets and knee pads. b) Rock Climbing - Offers adrenaline rush and builds strength. Join a local climbing gym to learn basics safely before transitioning outdoors. Invest in proper shoes, chalk bag, and harness. c) Soccer - A popular team sport promoting camaraderie. Find a recreational league near you; most leagues supply uniforms and balls. Shin guards, cleats, and comfortable clothing are essential. d) Running - One of the simplest forms of exercise requiring minimal investment. Buy good running shoes and start slow, increasing distance over time. Stay hydrated and consider using apps for tracking progress. Key Tip: Select something enjoyable to ensure consistency. Step 2: Learn Mechanical Skills Mechanical skills allow self-sufficiency and satisfaction derived from fixing things oneself. Here are some ideas: a) Car Repair - Familiarize yourself with basic car maintenance tasks like changing oil or replacing wiper blades. Over time, advance towards complex repairs after gaining confidence and knowledge. b) Woodworking - Building furniture or small wooden items enhances creativity and problem-solving abilities. Begin with simple projects and upgrade tools as needed. Remember always to prioritize safety. c) Bicycle Maintenance - Regularly cleaning and lubricating chains keeps bicycles functioning smoothly. As proficiency grows, attempt brake adjustments and tire changes. Guideline: Never hesitate to seek professional advice when unsure about any procedure. Step 3: Engage With Technology Technology offers numerous possibilities for exploration. Try these options: a) Programming - Learning coding languages opens up career opportunities and personal fulfillment. Numerous online resources exist for beginners including free courses and interactive platforms. b) Robotics - Constructing robots fosters innovation and critical thinking. Kits designed specifically for newcomers make entry into robotics less daunting. c) Gaming - Video games develop strategic planning and hand-eye coordination. Consoles or computers typically required; however, mobile gaming also presents viable alternatives. Key Tip: Keep learning updated since tech fields evolve rapidly. Step 4: Explore Other Interests There are countless other tomboy hobbies beyond what we've covered here. Some additional suggestions might include fishing, hunting, martial arts, or even amateur radio operation. Research widely until you find activities aligning with your interests and passions. Conclusion: Embracing tomboy hobbies allows individuals to challenge stereotypes, expand skillsets, and discover new passions. By following this comprehensive guide, readers should feel empowered to try out different activities and cultivate their unique identities. Happy exploring! Pigs Will Be Pigs by Amy Axelrod The students will add, subtract, and multiply amounts of money Lesson Created by: Stacy Aschenbrenner Bergman Elementary, Manhattan, KS - Pigs Will Be Pigs by Amy Axelrod - The Story of Money by Betsy Maestro - Money, Money, Money by Nancy Winslow Parker - Handout: "Pigs Money" - Handout: "Menu" - Notebook paper 1. Launching the Lesson - Ask the students to write down the name of as many denominations of money they can think. - Discuss the denominations. Share all of the correct answers. - Ask the students "Who is found of the penny?" "Who is on the nickel?" Continue to do this for the rest of the denominations just for fun. When finished, have the students share their answers. (Answers can be found in the book Money, Money, Money by Nancy Winslow Parker.) - Share some highlight about the history of money found in the book The Story of Money by Betsy Maestro. - Now read the book Pigs Will Be Pigs by Amy Axelrod. Explain to the kids that they are going to figure out exactly how much money the Pigs found. 2. Developing the Lesson - Give each student a "Pig's Money" handout. Explain to the students that they are to record how many coins or bills the pigs find. Each time money is found the students mark the amount of the handout. - When the story is finished, the students are to add and record the amount in each row. Then calculate the total amount of money found altogether. - Hand out a "Menu" to each student. Ask the students to calculate the cost of four Dinner Specials. - Now ask the students: - How much money did the Pigs find on their hunt? ($34.67) - How much did the Pigs pay for their dinner? ($31.96) - After dinner at Enchanted Enchilada, how much money did the Pigs have left? ($2.71) - What could the Pigs have bought (from the menu) with the money they had left over? (Answers may vary: Texas Caviar, Coffee, Flan Custard, and Cola, etc.) e. Ask the students to look at the menu and pick out their favorite foods. Let the students "spend" up to $15.00 on a dinner including food, drink, and dessert. Encourage the students to record their menu choices and calculate the amount of money they spent, as well as possible change. Let the students compare their results. - Talk about sales tax and tips. - How they are figured - Why they exist - Figure sales tax and a tip on the amount of money that the students spent when they had $15.00 to spend. (Do this even though the menu says that tax and tip are included in the price.) No you height tell me to take the other one [phone]. Or she'll sit in her highchair and play with some toys. Title: How to Deal With a Best Friend Stealer Introduction: Dealing with a best friend stealer can be challenging and emotionally draining. A best friend stealer is someone who tries to insert themselves into your friendship, often leading to feelings of betrayal and confusion. This article will provide you with a comprehensive guide on how to deal with this situation while maintaining your relationships and self-esteem. Remember that every situation is unique, so tailor these steps as needed to fit yours. Step 1: Identify the Situation Determine if the person's actions genuinely constitute an attempt at stealing your best friend or if there's another explanation. Key indicators include increased time spent together, one-on-one activities excluding you, sharing secrets without inviting you, or attempts to create distance between you and your friend. Once you have identified the issue, proceed to the next step. Step 2: Reflect on Your Feelings Acknowledge your emotions regarding the situation. You may feel hurt, betrayed, angry, or confused. Understanding and accepting your feelings will help you communicate effectively and make informed decisions moving forward. It's crucial to process these emotions before addressing the issue directly. Step 3: Communicate Openly with Your Best Friend Discuss your concerns privately with your best friend. Use "I" statements instead of blaming language (e.g., "I feel left out when..."). Explain the behaviors that concern you and share your feelings about them. Listen actively to their perspective and work collaboratively towards a solution. Maintain open communication throughout the entire process. Step 4: Address the Third Party Together If your best friend acknowledges your concerns and agrees that the third party's behavior is problematic, consider addressing the issue together. Approach the individual respectfully and express your concerns calmly. Emphasize the importance of group dynamics and invite them to participate in inclusive activities rather than exclusive ones. Be prepared for potential defensiveness but remain composed and assertive. Step 5: Set Boundaries Establish clear boundaries within your social circle. Clearly define what behaviors are acceptable and unacceptable among friends. Encourage inclusivity and discourage exclusivity by setting up group activities and fostering positive interactions between all parties involved. Consistently enforce these boundaries to maintain a healthy environment. Step 6: Seek Outside Perspective Speak with other trusted friends or family members about the situation. They might offer valuable insights and advice based on their experiences. Additionally, they could serve as objective mediators during conversations involving the three parties. Step 7: Practice Self-Care Managing such situations can take a toll on your mental health. Engage in regular self-care practices like exercise, meditation, or hobbies to reduce stress and anxiety. Surround yourself with supportive people who uplift and encourage you. Focusing on personal wellbeing allows you to approach the situation more constructively. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Stay Calm: Keeping your cool amidst conflict preserves relationships and encourages productive dialogue. * Avoid Blame Game: Place responsibility where it belongs - focusing on specific behaviors rather than assigning blame maintains trust and understanding. * Prioritize Honesty: Authenticity builds strong foundations for resolving issues and strengthening bonds. * Foster Positive Interactions: Promote positivity amongst friends to minimize tension and nurture genuine connections. * Learn from Past Experiences: Apply lessons learned from previous encounters to current challenges for growth and maturity. Don't Grab the Ants, /a/! A Beginning Reading Lesson Rationale: Students will become familiar with the short vowel correspondence a=/a/. Children must be able to map out work spellings to successfully read This lesson engages children in hands on instruction with a letterbox lesson, a decodable text, and various activities to provide practice with the short correspondence =/a/. Upon completion of this lesson students will be able to read and spell words containing short a. Materials: pencils, graphic image of a crying baby, cover up critters for each student, whiteboard or smart board, magnetic letters if using whiteboard, paper letter tiles for each student with letters: a, b, c, d, f, g, h, k, l, m, p, r, s, t, chart paper with the words: rag, mat, flag, had, pack, bass, mask, clap, fact, grab, strap written on it, assessment worksheet http://www.funfonix.com/book1/ffonix_book1_3.gif , copy of Ants In A Can for each student. 1. Teacher says: "Before we can become good readers we need to learn the alphabetic code that tells us how to pronounce the words. Today, we are going to learn how to spell and read words that have a short a in them." 2. Teacher says: "We have already learned the sound a short a makes. Can anybody tell me what "short a" sounds like? Remember to raise you hand before answering. That's right, a=/a/! Lets pull out our crying baby picture that helps us remember a=/a/". [Show graphic image]. 3. Teacher says: "To get us warmed up lets practice listening for the /a/ sound in some words. That smells bad! When I said the word bad I noticed that I opened my mouth wide like a crying baby. B-aaa-d, bad. Yes, there is a short a in bad. No I want you to try some. If you hear the /a/ sound I want you to open your mouth wide like you are a crying baby. Don't make any sound just move your mouth. Is it is boy, at, pet, map, trap, fab, vest?" [Observe the class while they are opening their mouth to monitor who hears this phoneme]. 4. Teacher says: "Now, what if I want to spell the word rag. She wiped up the spill with a rag. A rag in this sentence is used as a towel to clean up a mess. Hmm, first I think I will stretch the word out to listen to the sounds I hear. Rrrr-aaaa-gggg, rag. Now I am going to count the sounds I hear, /r/ /a/ /g/, 3 sounds. I am going to pull out three letterboxes to spell this word. [Either draw letterboxes on the whiteboard, or pull them up on the smart board]. I know I heard my mouth open wide like a baby, so I know the /a/ sound is in there. I heard the /a/ right before the /g/, so I am going to write a short a in the 2nd box. It is easiest to start when you hear the vowel sound. Rag starts with /r/, so I am going to put an r in the first box. Now there is one box left, so I am going to say my word again, rag. The letter I have missing is g, the /g/ sound, so I am going to place my g in the 3rd box. 5. Teacher says: "Lets try another word. I need three volunteers to help me spell the word mat. The mat was on the floor. Remember to count the sounds, /m/ /a/ /t/. How many letterboxes do we need? [Wait for response]. 3, good! Who can come to the board and write the letter that goes in the first box? Who can come write the letter that goes in the second box? The third? Great job!" 6. Teacher says: Now, I want you to spell some words in your letterboxes. I am going to call out a word, and I want you to spell it. I will walk around the room to check spellings. Lets try a 4-phoneme word, flag. For each sound you hear put the letter in the box. Listen hard for the beginning sound. Flag, There was a flag waving outside. Flag. Who wants to come put their spelling in the boxes on the whiteboard? Good job!" Repeat this process for each new word. Remind students to stretch the words out, and listen for the baby crying a=/a/ sound. [3-phoneme: had, pack, bass], [4-phoneme: mask, clap, fact, grab], [5-phoneme: strap] 7. Teacher says: "Now I am going to show you how I would read this word. [Pull out chart paper with words listed on them]. First I see the a in the middle so it must say /a/ like our crying baby. I'm going to use my cover up critter to read the rest of the word. [Cover up and blend before the vowel, and then with the vowel]. /r/ /a/ = /ra/. Now I am going to blend /ra/ with the "g" on the end. /ra/ /g/, /rag/. Oh, that's easy! Rag, like, I need a rag to clean up this mess. Now I want everybody to look at our chart paper, and let's read the words together. [rag, mat, flag, had, pack, bass, mask, clap, fact, grab, strap] Great job everyone! 8. Pull out the flashcards. Mix the words up, and ask children to raise their hand to read the word. Ask them to only read one word, so that everybody can have a turn. Have students read the flashcards until every child has had at least one turn. 9. Teacher says: Now, we are going to read the book Ants in a Can. This book is about a little girl named Jan who tries to touch an ant. Her dad tells her to put the ant in the can, so she tries to get the ant by giving it a snack. The ants hurry to the snack, and Jan hits them! What do you think will happen to Jan? Will the ants hurt her? We will have to read to find out! I want everybody to read to the person sitting beside you. You can move around the room so you can hear each other if you want to. I want you to read a page, and then let your partner read a page. If you get stuck on a word use your cover up critter, or look at our phoneme picture of the crying baby! I will be walking around the room if you need help!" 10. Teacher says: Great job reading! What snack did the ants have? [Wait for response]. What did the ants do to Jan? [Wait for response]. What did Jan's Dad give her at the end of the story? [Wait for response]. 11. Give each student the assessment worksheet. http://www.funfonix.com/book1/ffonix_book1_3.gif Say: In this worksheet you will need to look at the pictures and read the words in the word bank. Pick the word that matches the picture and print it in the spaces under the pictures. You can use your cover up critter to read the words if you need help. Hello young learners! Today, we are going to talk about a fascinating concept called "Solving Equations with Shapes". This is like a fun puzzle where we use shapes and their arrangements to find solutions to mathematical problems. Imagine you have a big box full of different shaped blocks. Some are squares, triangles, rectangles, or even circles! Your task is to arrange these blocks according to certain rules so that you can find a special solution. Let's see how this works using a simple example. First, let's say we want to organize our blocks following these steps: 1. Make two lines with the blocks. One horizontal line at the top and one vertical line at the side. These lines will represent the shape of our final arrangement. 2. Place square blocks along the horizontal line. We need exactly three square blocks here. 3. Now put triangle blocks below each square block in the vertical line. So if there are three square blocks, we also need three triangle blocks. 4. Finally, ensure that the total number of dots (or vertices) on both lines remains equal. In other words, the sum of dots on the horizontal line should match the sum of dots on the vertical line.
